title: Corporate Services Administrator
description: **The Role** WPS is currently seeking a Corporate Services Administrator to join our dynamic and growing team in Dublin. In this role, you will work with a senior manager to oversee the compliance and regulatory obligations of our transactions and assist with the delivery of internal projects. This is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in a highly regarded and expanding organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous professional development and career progression within a collegiate team\-based environment. **Duties \& Responsibilities** You will have a broad range of responsibilities including: * Assist with the management of the quarterly board meeting cycle for a large number of SPV's; * Monitor regulatory and compliance deliverables and assist with ensuring these deliverables are met; * Prepare reports and presentations to assist the management team; * Assist with the delivery of internal projects and initiatives; * Ensure that key transaction data is recorded and kept up\-to\-date on internal records; * Oversee the administration of various data bases including internal data bases and external banking platforms; * Assist with various company secretarial tasks including company incorporations, annual compliance, filings including annual returns with the Companies Registration Office and other ad hoc duties; * Arrange board meeting logistics including agreeing board calendars, sending calendar invitations and diarising agreed dates with Board of Directors; * Assist the senior management with various administrative tasks, including arranging events, client meetings etc.; * Assist the team with managing disbursements and invoicing clients; * Demonstrates awareness and understanding of legal or regulatory developments and proactively seeks to ensure that the team is positioned to address those challenges; * compliance with internal policies and procedures, while actively contributing to process improvements within the team **Knowledge \& Experience** * A minimum of three years' experience in a relevant legal, financial services or corporate administration role is essential; * A university law or business graduate, with an excellent academic background; * A desire to work in a client facing environment where delivery of a professional service to both clients and other counterparties is of key importance; * Strong communication skills, an ability to think on their feet, excellent attention to detail and exceptional organisational skills are essential for this multi\-client role; * Completion of or currently studying for the company secretarial qualification (Grad ICSA) would be an advantage; * Experience with Viewpoint, Power BI and Oracle would be useful, but it is not essential. * Conscientious, organised and a team player who can work on their own and as part of a wider team; and * Enthusiastic in nature, with a real interest in learning and developing their skills. **Benefits** We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Discretionary annual bonus scheme * 23 days annual leave * Hybrid Working Policy – 3 days in the office * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme * Life Assurance * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees * Social Committee * Employee Assistance Programme * Wellness programme

title: Credit Analyst- Graduate (Dublin)
description: **About Wayflyer** Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer. Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan. You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney. **The Challenge** Wayflyer moves fast. We fund eCommerce businesses at the speed they need to grow \- and to do that responsibly, we need people who can look beyond a spreadsheet and see the business behind the numbers. This role exists because great funding decisions aren't just about data \- they're about judgement. We need someone who can develop both. **What You'll Actually Do** * Dig into the story behind the data \- analyze the financial and non\-financial performance of new and existing customers to understand what's really driving their business * Learn to assess and quantify risk \- get under the hood of eCommerce businesses and figure out how Wayflyer can fund their growth without taking on unnecessary exposure * Get on the phone \- join regular customer calls to understand their business models, funding needs, and where they're headed * Work with management to build and test new processes and products, not just inherit them * Support sales and operations by understanding how they win, and what they need to keep customer relationships healthy * Get across our loan portfolio \- understand how we manage it, what good looks like, and how we make the call to exit a risky relationship * Connect dots across the business \- this role touches a lot of teams, and we expect you to use that access **What This Role Could Turn Into** Analysts at Wayflyer don't stay analysts for long if they don't want to. This function sits at the intersection of finance, risk, and commercial strategy \- people who come in here have gone on to lead underwriting decisions, manage portfolio strategy, and step into senior roles across the business. The trajectory is real, and it moves fast. **Who Thrives Here** You're the kind of person who reads a set of accounts and immediately wants to know what's *behind* them. You ask questions most people wouldn't think to ask. You're comfortable presenting a recommendation in a room where people will push back \- and you welcome that. You're not looking for a narrow lane. You want to understand the whole picture. You probably haven't done this exact job before. That's fine. We're hiring for how you think, not what you've already done. **We Hire for Range** * You've completed (or are close to completing) an undergraduate degree \- Business, Finance, Economics, Engineering, Law, or Computer Science all work here * You have a strong academic record, and you can speak to why * You're genuinely curious about credit, risk, and what makes an eCommerce business tick * You're comfortable leading a conversation \- in a meeting, on a call, in a presentation Bonus points if you can read a set of management accounts, work with Excel or SQL, or are planning to pursue a professional finance qualification. None of these are dealbreakers \- they're just a head start. **Location and working policy** **📍** Dublin HQ, hybrid (3\-4 days per week in the office) **The good stuff** 25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and a pension. Equity \- because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental leave for primary and secondary caregivers. 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want. 💡 **How We Handle Your Personal Data** By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can contact our privacy team at privacy@wayflyer.com or review our privacy notice at https://wayflyer.com/privacy\-notice.

title: Critical Environment Technician Intern
description: **Overview** As a Critical Environment Technician Intern (CET Intern) in Microsoft’s Cloud Operations \& Innovation (CO\+I) team, you will work with the CE Day Team in a learning capacity to maintain the critical infrastructure that keeps our Datacenters up and running. This could be anything from coordinating with supplier/vendors, recognizing safe versus unsafe work environment, having a hands\-on understanding of how critical environment equipment works, performing various types of maintenance, responding to onsite incidents while coordinating with other critical facilities professionals, and using telemetry and other platforms to monitor equipment performance and operations. In alignment with our Microsoft values, we are committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for all employees to positively impact our culture every day and we need you as a Critical Environment Technician Intern (CET Intern) Microsoft’s Cloud Operations \& Innovation (CO\+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a CO\+I DCT Intern, you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO\+I is focused on the personal and professional development for all employees and offers trainings and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity \& Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications. Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 100 datacenters and 1 million servers. Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide. With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our datacenter design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever\-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world\-class cloud provider. Do you want to empower billions across the world? Come and join us in CO\+I and be at the forefront of the action! **Responsibilities** * Serve as an Intern on the Critical Environment Day Team in the electrical area, working on basic tasks independently and working on more advanced tasks together with experienced CE Technicians in a learning capacity. * Learn to Inspect and supervise critical environment\-related facility equipment (e.g., controls, heating, ventilation, and air conditioning \[HVAC], mechanical systems), building, and grounds regularly for unsafe or abnormal conditions and escalate per applicable policies and standards. * Learn to utilize internal computerized maintenance management system (CMMS) to track all equipment assets and to complete work order requests for maintenance work. * Safely and quickly respond to and support onsite incident response teams for all abnormal conditions that impact operations, and coordinates with other critical facilities professionals to perform corrective repairs, with supervision. * Learn to follow preexisting emergency operating procedures (EOPs), methods of procedure (MOPs), and standard operating procedures (SOPs) in relation to CE operations and incidents. * Under oversight, perform various types of maintenance (e.g., planned, predictive, corrective) and repairs following methods of procedure (MOPs), and standard operating procedures (SOPs) for one or more types of equipment (e.g., electrical, mechanical, cooling systems), correct procedures. * Learn by shadowing and training through our In\-house program a hands\-on understanding of how equipment works and how to troubleshoot equipment, systems, subsystems, and components independently within their trained discipline i.e. Electrical. * Understands, follows, and ensures safety and security requirements (e.g., job hazard assessments \[JHAs], environmental health and safety (EH\&S), toolbox talks), and business processes and procedures to properly perform work in a safe, quality, and reliable within Microsoft requirements. * Move equipment using pallet jacks or other assisted lifting equipment. * Embody our culture and values **Qualifications** **Required Qualifications:** * High School Diploma, or equivalent such as Leaving Certificate. * Qualified Electrician holding a National Craft Certificate in Electrical Installation. * You need to be an Irish National or, have residency and you must be eligible to work in Ireland. **Preferred Qualifications** * Ability to support frequent standing, walking, lifting, and working at heights with or without equipment assistance. * Able to communicate effectively with multiple stakeholders. * Proficient in written and oral English communication to support regional and global teams. * Desire to learn, grow, and develop new technical skills. * Flexibility to work non\-standard business hours that may include weekends and/or holidays. This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled. Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about **requesting accommodations.**

title: Critical Facilities Technician
description: ****About Vantage Data Centers**** Vantage Data Centers powers, cools, protects and connects the technology of the world’s well\-known hyperscalers, cloud providers and large enterprises. Developing and operating across North America, EMEA and Asia Pacific, Vantage has evolved data center design in innovative ways to deliver dramatic gains in reliability, efficiency and sustainability in flexible environments that can scale as quickly as the market demands. ****About Vantage Data Centers**** Vantage Data Centers powers, cools, protects and connects the technology of the world’s well\-known hyperscalers, cloud providers and large enterprises. Developing and operating across North America, EMEA and Asia Pacific, Vantage has evolved data center design in innovative ways to deliver dramatic gains in reliability, efficiency and sustainability in flexible environments that can scale as quickly as the market demands. **Site Operations Department** The Site Operations Department is responsible for the overall health of all mechanical and electrical systems, building maintenance, and performance of all data modules in our data centers. The team is staffed 24 x 7 onsite with Critical Facilities Engineers (CFEs) working on shift and reporting to a Chief Engineer. This team is the face of Vantage to our customers and the guarantee of business continuity and excellence of Operations of our campus as well as the performance of our customer installations. Using a Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) to plan and track maintenance activities, the Site Operations Department is responsible for all preventative and corrective maintenance. Our culture expects and rewards team performance that engages directly with the customers and the facility infrastructure. ****Position Overview**** The successful candidate for this Critical Facilities Technician role knows what best\-in\-class data center service looks like and can make that a reality. This role is an important member of our Operations team where we manage some of the most efficient and cost\-effective data center facilities in the industry. Persons this role will work with a team of other electrical and mechanical\-focused engineers performing maintenance and repair for our data center infrastructure. We take our customer requirements for uptime seriously and keep our facilities maintained to a very high standard to insure uninterrupted service for our customers. If you enjoy working on critical data center systems (UPSs, PDUs, Diesel Generators, ATS equipment, and Building Controls), as well as industrial electrical equipment and systems that include power distribution components/wiring, electronic components, and lighting systems. If you know the importance of getting it right the first time and not disrupting mission\-critical data centers. If you enjoy delighting customers with superior service and you have the desire and ability to do so, then please read on! ****Essential Job Functions**** * Operate, monitor, maintain, and respond to abnormal conditions in facility systems. Areas includes: Electrical, mechanical, building monitoring, and control * Ensure the completion of maintenance activities to the highest quality level of planification, preparation, execution, and reporting * Ensure the full reliability of the infrastructures with the focus on continuity of services and energy efficiency * Use the BMS (Building Management System) and all available systems to appropriate monitoring the site including regular walkthroughs verifying the good working condition **Duties** * Work in shifts, per assigned shift pattern and wear personal protecting clothes depending on the work to be performed * Within applicable and authorized interventions according to the local regulation and the up\-to\-date certification(s) of the Critical Facilities Technician, be capable of manipulating / activating certain electrical and or mechanical and or security infrastructures for the purpose of protecting people and property and / or maintaining service continuity on the site * Use of the Building Management System (BMS) and regular onsite checks * With the assistance of the IT and Controls departments, ensure the proper operation of the site BMS * In close collaboration with the contractors, participate in the drafting of the preventive maintenance schedule for the site infrastructures and implement it in collaboration with all service providers concerned. Closely monitor the operations with a continuous concern for minimizing risks during interventions, and ensure the reporting of this activity * Operational management of suppliers and service providers, to document and plan preventive maintenance and or repair activities. Using a Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) and the Change Management Process. * Closely monitor the work of vendors with a continuous concern for minimizing risks during interventions, ensuring cleanliness standard, and ensure proper reporting of the performed activity * Perform repair and maintenance activities as required * Implementation of improvement plans and small projects * Participate in the implementation of the site\-specific crisis management plan and in the tests and implementation exercises concerning this plan * Participate in regular site audits and reviews, which are designed to assess the level of service continuity and analyze risks ****Job Requirements**** * Technical education (advanced technician or engineer level) in one of the following areas: Electricity (generators, UPS, low voltage distribution), climate, and cooling. * Experience in operating data centers or similar environments * Versatile technical knowledge, especially in the areas of operation, maintenance, and inspection of critical systems * English language – The minimum level required is B1 (intermediate, able to understand and communicate about familiar topics and handle most everyday situations independently) * Knowledge of fire protection and physical security (access control, video, and intrusion detection systems) or central building management systems is advantageous. * Good communication skills with third parties and internal customers * Highly motivated at problem\-solving * Attention to detail and a genuine commitment to following up on actions * Good writing skills * Everyday use of standard office equipment * Proven skills in the use of software, such as BMS, Computerized Maintenance Management Systems * Potential employees must complete a background check successfully ****Physical Activities And Requirements**** * Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles and the like * Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position\-to\-position. * Raised floor work. Must be able to maneuver and work underneath and around raised floor of varying heights. We operate with No Ego and No Arrogance. We work to build each other up and support one another, appreciating each other’s strengths and respecting each other’s weaknesses. We find joy in our work and each other, actively seeking opportunities to inject fun into what we do. Our hard and efficient work is rewarded with an above market total compensation package. We offer a comprehensive suite of health and welfare, retirement, and paid leave benefits exceeding local expectations. Throughout the year, the advantage of being part of the Vantage team is evident with an array of benefits, recognition, training and development, and the knowledge that your contribution adds value to the company and our community. Don't meet all the requirements? Please still apply if you think you are the right person for the position. We are always keen to speak to people who connect with our mission and values. Vantage Data Centers is an Equal Opportunity Employer Vantage Data Centers does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firm agencies. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired; such resumes will be deemed the sole property of Vantage Data Centers. We operate with No Ego and No Arrogance. We work to build each other up and support one another, appreciating each other’s strengths and respecting each other’s weaknesses. We find joy in our work and each other, actively seeking opportunities to inject fun into what we do. Our hard and efficient work is rewarded with an above market total compensation package. We offer a comprehensive suite of health and welfare, retirement, and paid leave benefits exceeding local expectations. Throughout the year, the advantage of being part of the Vantage team is evident with an array of benefits, recognition, training and development, and the knowledge that your contribution adds value to the company and our community. Don't meet all the requirements? Please still apply if you think you are the right person for the position. We are always keen to speak to people who connect with our mission and values. Vantage Data Centers is an Equal Opportunity Employer Vantage Data Centers does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firm agencies. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired; such resumes will be deemed the sole property of Vantage Data Centers.

title: Customer Engineer, SMB, Artificial Intelligence
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI\-related technical field, or equivalent practical experience. * 4 years of experience with cloud native architecture in a customer\-facing or support role. * Experience engaging with, or presenting to, technical stakeholders or executive leaders. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer or Professional Data Engineer certification (in addition to GCP Professional Cloud Architect/Associate Customer Engineer). * Experience prospecting, and building and maintaining customer relationships from scratch, inspiring customers with the "art of the possible" using AI. * Experience with AI, machine learning frameworks, or data science platforms. * Experience engaging with, or presenting to, technical stakeholders or executive leaders. * Deep passion for continuous learning in the AI space, with a demonstrated ability to translate AI concepts into practical, revenue\-generating business solutions. **About the job** ----------------- The Google Cloud Platform team helps customers transform and build what's next for their business — all with technology built in the cloud. Our products are developed for security, reliability and scalability, running the full stack from infrastructure to applications to devices and hardware. Our teams are dedicated to helping our customers — developers, small and large businesses, educational institutions and government agencies — see the benefits of our technology come to life. As part of an entrepreneurial team in this rapidly growing business, you will play a key role in understanding the needs of our customers and help shape the future of businesses of all sizes use technology to connect with customers, employees and partners. When leading companies choose Google Cloud it's a huge win for spreading the power of cloud computing globally. Google Cloud accelerates organizations' ability to digitally transform their business with the best infrastructure, platform, industry solutions and expertise. We deliver enterprise\-grade solutions that leverage Google's all on the cleanest cloud in the industry. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to solve their most critical business problems. As a part of our specialized AI Customer Engineering team, you will be at the forefront of the AI revolution. Your unique mandate empowers you to advocate the entire Google Cloud Platform portfolio while deeply specializing in AI capabilities. In this role, you will continuously hone your AI/ML expertise, acting as a visionary who seamlessly integrates AI into comprehensive cloud architectures to drive unprecedented growth for our SMB customers.Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise\-grade solutions that leverage Google’s technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €80000 \- €82000 (EUR) \+ 42\.86% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Prepare and deliver product messaging that highlights the Google Cloud value proposition using techniques such as slide presentations, product demonstrations, white papers, and documents. * Own the technical aspects of solutions, including providing high\-impact Google Cloud Platform (GCP)/AI product demonstrations, and briefing customer executives on AI\-driven business transformations. * Dedicate continuous time to mastering Google’s latest AI technologies, maintaining a razor\-sharp edge to act as the ultimate AI authority for customers. * Act as the strategic technical advisor for SMB customers, architecting holistic GCP solutions with AI and Machine Learning at their core. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. title: Customer Success Executive
description: At **Alkimii** , we’re on a mission to transform how hospitality businesses manage their people and property operations. Our platform helps hotels run smoother operations, improve team communication and create better workplaces for their employees. As we continue to grow, we’re looking for a **Customer Success Executive** who enjoys helping people, solving problems and delivering outstanding service to our customers. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a fast\-paced environment and wants to develop their career in a growing SaaS company serving the hospitality industry. **What you’ll be doing** As part of our Customer Success team, you’ll be the **first point of contact for our customers** , supporting hospitality teams as they use Alkimii to manage their daily operations. In this role you will: • Respond to customer queries via phone and email in a timely and friendly manner • Troubleshoot issues and guide customers through solutions using the Alkimii platform • Ensure every interaction reflects the high standards of service our customers expect • Work closely with the wider team to improve the customer experience • Help customers get the most value from the platform by understanding how they use it in their operations **What success looks like in your first months** • You feel confident supporting customers and navigating the Alkimii platform • Customers receive quick, helpful and friendly responses to their queries • You build strong relationships with customers and become a trusted point of contact • You contribute ideas to improve the support experience and internal processes **We think you’ll thrive in this role if you:** • Have previous experience in customer service or a customer\-facing role • Have experience working in hospitality or enjoy working with hospitality businesses • Are organised and comfortable managing multiple conversations and priorities • Communicate clearly and enjoy helping people solve problems • Are proactive, curious and eager to learn new technology **Requirements** • Strong computer and technical skills • Fluent written and spoken English • Excellent communication and attention to detail • Eligibility to work full\-time in Ireland **What you can expect from us** 💰Full time competitive salary 💲Clevercards \- Employee Benefits 🩺 Wellbeing programs, learning and growth opportunities 🧘 ♀️ Flexible working week, because we take seriously the productivity and wellbeing of our team 🚀 You will work on meaningful and challenging tasks in a relaxed yet supportive environment. 🙋 4,000 Employee Referral Program 🌅 Work in a beautiful building located on Skerries Harbour overlooking the pier! we get the best sunsets! 👯 Quarterly team meet\-ups \& annual gathering **Why join Alkimii?** We’re a team that cares deeply about our customers and about each other. Our culture is built on **kindness, curiosity and ownership** , and we’re always looking for ways to improve how we work and how we support hospitality teams around the world. If you enjoy solving problems, helping people and being part of a growing company where your ideas matter, we’d love to hear from you. *Before you apply, we feel it’s important that you know a bit more about us…* We: * Enjoy being up early enough to see the sunrise * Appreciate a good barista coffee * Discipline ourselves with morning workouts * Feed our curiosity with a good book * Don’t take ourselves too seriously * Constantly ask ourselves how we can make it easier for our customers * Love long walks on the beach (yes, really!) All sounding good? Check out what your new commute might look like: https://goo.gl/maps/cf5LPcJmCBBPYZFR8

title: Cyber Security Engineer
description: I am looking to speak with established Cyber Security Engineers specialising in Incident Response to join my clients growing security operations function. This is a key hire and represents a great opportunity for career growth, development and challenge in a Global business. South Dublin with a hybrid 3 days onsite expectation You will lead the investigation, containment, and resolution of complex, high\-impact security incidents, working closely with incident response leadership and cross\-functional IT and security teams. Strengthen the organisation’s security posture through proactive **threat hunting and detection engineering** , using insights from incidents, threat intelligence, and security assessments to continuously improve SOC capabilities. This is a hands\-on technical role requiring strong analytical capability, sound judgement, and a collaborative approach in a global environment. The role includes participation in an on\-call rotation and regular on\-site presence for onboarding, sensitive activities, and team collaboration. **Responsibilities** * Act as senior escalation point for complex cybersecurity incidents including ransomware, phishing, account compromise, insider threats, data leakage, and third\-party incidents * Lead end\-to\-end incident investigations across SOC, infrastructure, IT, vulnerability management, threat intelligence, and application security teams * Collect and analyse data from host, network, identity, cloud, and third\-party sources to determine scope, impact, and root cause * Manage incident lifecycle from detection through containment, eradication, recovery, and post\-incident review * Document incidents clearly and communicate updates and recommendations to technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Improve SOC processes, including MTTR, KPIs, and SLOs * Support and participate in tabletop exercises and incident response readiness activities * Maintain and enhance playbooks, procedures, and detection workflows * Conduct proactive threat hunting to identify vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and emerging threats * Research attacker TTPs and IOCs relevant to the organisation and industry * Develop and refine detection logic, scripts, and queries across SIEM/EDR platforms * Translate threat intelligence into actionable detections and tune for accuracy and reduced false positives * Collaborate with Security Engineering to deploy and optimise detection content * Leverage AI tools and security automation platforms to enhance analysis and detection capabilities **Requirements** * 5–8\+ years’ experience in cybersecurity, SOC operations, or incident response * Proven experience leading or contributing to major security incident investigations * Strong background in threat hunting and detection engineering * Hands\-on experience with SIEM, EDR, IDS/IPS, and forensic tools * Experience working in cloud environments (AWS and/or Azure) and identity systems (Active Directory / Azure AD) * Strong understanding of incident response methodologies and security frameworks * Relevant certifications such as GCIH, GCFA, CISSP (or equivalent) are desirable **Must be in Ireland, Must Hold Stamp 4, EU of Irish Passport** Email: shane.doolin@realtime.jobs for more detail

company: HSE Dublin and North East Recruitment
description: We welcome enquiries about the role. **Informal Enquiries for the role:** For further information about the post please contact **HR Enquiries:** Integrated Health Areas of Dublin North County and Dublin North City \& West is committed to providing information and services which are accessible to all, if you have a recruitment related query or require accessibility assistance throughout any stage of the recruitment process, please contact: nicola.hutchinson@hse.ie **Details of Service** A team of dedicated paediatric physiotherapists provide a primary care physiotherapy service to children under the age of 18 years at community healthcare network level across the CHO. title: Data Annotation and Quality Specialist
description: Akara Robotics are currently hiring for a Data Annotation and Quality Specialist to join their team. **About The Role:** =================== We are looking for a Data Annotation and Quality Specialist to support the development and quality assurance of Akara’s AI products. Working closely with Akara’s AI and operations teams, as well as external clinical partners, you will review, interpret, and annotate operating room data from a variety of sources, including video footage, system\-generated data streams, graphical plots, and clinical records. You will identify relevant clinical and operational events, assess the accuracy of system\-generated outputs, and ensure that annotated data consistently meets clearly defined quality standards. The role will initially focus on hands\-on data annotation, validation, and quality assurance. As Akara’s annotation function grows, you will have the opportunity to take on broader responsibilities in process improvement, training, workflow coordination, and quality management, with the potential to progress into a team leadership position. This role is well suited to someone who enjoys taking ownership, solving practical problems, and working in a fast\-moving environment where their contribution will have a clear and visible impact. **Key Responsibilities:** ------------------------- * Review and annotate operating room data, including video, sensor outputs, graphical plots, and clinical records * Identify relevant clinical and operational events using established annotation guidelines * Review system\-generated data and flag errors, inconsistencies, or uncertain cases * Perform quality checks to ensure annotations are accurate, complete, and consistent * Compare information across multiple data sources to resolve discrepancies * Maintain clear and accurate records of completed annotation work * Follow data handling, privacy, and information security procedures * Escalate ambiguous or complex cases for further review * Contribute feedback on annotation guidelines, tools, and quality assurance processes * Support the development of more efficient and scalable annotation workflows * As the team grows, assist with training, mentoring, and coordinating other annotators **What Success Looks Like:** ---------------------------- During your first 12 months, you will: * Consistently produce accurate and reliable annotations * Meet agreed productivity and quality targets * Develop a strong understanding of Akara’s annotation standards and operating room workflows * Identify recurring data quality issues and contribute to their resolution * Help improve annotation guidance, quality controls, and review processes * Support the onboarding and training of new annotation team members * Take increasing ownership of annotation projects and workflows **Key Requirements:** --------------------- * Exceptional attention to detail and the ability to complete repetitive analytical tasks with a high degree of accuracy * Ability to follow detailed instructions, guidelines, and quality standards consistently * Ability to interpret visual information and identify patterns or discrepancies across multiple data sources * Strong computer literacy and proficiency with standard software applications * Good organisational and time\-management skills * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work independently while contributing effectively within a team * Eligibility to work in Ireland without requiring visa sponsorship **Desirable Qualifications and Experience:** -------------------------------------------- * Previous experience in data annotation, data labelling, quality assurance, data review, auditing, or a similar analytical role * A bachelor’s degree or equivalent qualification in a STEM discipline, health sciences, biomedical engineering, data science, computer science, or a related field * Previous experience annotating video, image, sensor, or healthcare\-related datasets * An understanding of clinical terminology, operating room processes, or healthcare workflows * Experience using annotation, labelling, or data\-quality management tools * Experience working with confidential, sensitive, or regulated data * Previous experience training, mentoring, or coordinating the work of others * Experience supervising or managing a small team **About Akara:** ================ Akara is a spin\-out from Trinity College that helps hospitals perform more procedures and reduce infections through faster and more efficient room decontamination, powered by cutting\-edge robotic automation and AI. This is important because patients that get infections spend three times longer in hospitals, and infections acquired in hospitals lead to four times more deaths than road traffic accidents. Akara is currently supported by Enterprise Ireland through their high potential startups program (HPSU). To date they have raised €3M in funding, including a €2\.5M grant from the European Innovation Council, Europe’s most prestigious deep\-tech funding program. They are currently raising a €2\.5M seed funding round.

title: Data Center Technician
description: ****Overview**** As a Microsoft Data Center Technician (DCT), you will develop an understanding of standard processes and procedures for preparing, installing, performing diagnostics, troubleshooting, replacing, and/or decommissioning equipment under the guidance of more experienced Data Center colleagues. This opportunity will allow you to develop an understanding of our Data Center environment, familiarize yourself with security and data management procedures, and accelerate your career growth in the process. Microsoft’s Cloud Operations \& Innovation (CO\+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a CO\+I DCT, you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO\+I is focused on the personal and professional development of all employees and offers training and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity \& Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications. Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 200 Data Centers in 32 countries and millions of servers. Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide. With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our data center design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever\-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world\-class cloud provider. Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond. ****Responsibilities**** * Contributes to issue tracking through escalations, asking questions, and seeking guidance on timely resolution of issues from more experienced technicians. * Participates in daily safety briefings, completes required training aligned to the role and workload including safety training, complies with safety procedures (e.g., equipment use, lifting, electrical hazards, ladder/rolling stair use). * Completes assigned tickets efficiently and in alignment with Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) while meeting established Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with guidance from other technicians. * Complies with security and data management procedures and policies with guidance from other technicians and completes the required security training. * Maintains a strong client focus to understand the impact of their work when completing tickets and assigned tasks. * Contributes to a positive and effective team environment by sharing information with others, contributing to regular team meetings, asking questions, and staying apprised of the status of others' work. * Has pride and a sense of accountability for the service quality, completeness, and ownership of the data center facilities. * Embodies our culture and values. ****Qualifications**** **Required** **Qualifications** * Completed High/Secondary School, GED, an apprenticeship/vocational qualification, or equivalent experience and basic knowledge of computer hardware and components. * You may be required to travel between sites as part of this role so a valid driver’s license will be required. While not required, we also look for the following **Preferred Qualifications:** * Experience supporting IT equipment or related technology. * Applicable certifications: CompTIA (A\+, Server\+, Network\+), Basic Structure Cabling (BSC). ****Background Check Requirements:**** Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings: * Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter. * As stated in the job posting, this position requires verification of citizenship or other protected status to meet legal requirements of the role. Data Center Technicians ATR\-B \- The typical base pay range for this role across Ireland is € 39,600\.00 \- € 49,100\.00 per year. Certain roles may be eligible for benefits and other compensation. **Find Additional Benefits And Pay Information Here:** https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/corporate\-pay/ireland\-corporate\-pay.html Data Center Technicians ATR\-C \- The typical base pay range for this role across Ireland is € 42,500\.00 \- € 61,000\.00 per year. Certain roles may be eligible for benefits and other compensation. **Find Additional Benefits And Pay Information Here:** https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/corporate\-pay/ireland\-corporate\-pay.html This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled. Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about **requesting accommodations.**

title: Data Scientist
description: **About the Company** Our team is expanding, and we are on the lookout for a talented Data Scientist to join our team and help improve neural networks for the analysis of satellite imagery. The primary responsibility will be to research, test, and enhance neural networks that classify terrain types and estimate the height of classified objects based on satellite images. The training data will be provided by our technical team. This position can be offered on a part\-time basis, making it an ideal opportunity for a researcher or academic professional looking to combine work with ongoing studies or research. The ideal candidates will collaboratively tackle technical challenges, make informed decisions, and work both independently and within a team setting. We value individuals with a thirst for learning, adept at adapting to evolving tasks swiftly. This role is perfect for those who are enthusiastic about data science and eager to work with cutting\-edge technologies to craft bespoke solutions for our clientele and internal processes. **About the Role** The primary responsibility will be to research, test, and enhance neural networks that classify terrain types and estimate the height of classified objects based on satellite images. **Responsibilities** * Research, develop, and optimize neural network architectures for satellite image analysis. * Conduct experiments to improve model accuracy and efficiency. * Collaborate with our technical team to ensure the quality and relevance of the training data. * Evaluate different models and approaches for terrain classification and object height estimation. * Continuously iterate and improve models based on performance metrics and project needs. **Qualifications** * Minimum degree Bachelor in a relevant field (e.g., Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, Image Processing). A PhD in these areas is preferred, but not required. **Required Skills** * Proven experience in developing and fine\-tuning neural networks for image classification tasks. * Strong background in machine learning, deep learning, and computer vision (must have). * Proficiency in Python, Keras, Matplotlib, Pandas. * Knowledge of TensorFlow, PyTorch and other relevant machine learning frameworks is a plus. **Preferred Skills** * Nice to have experience with QGIS, GDAL, Rasterio and Transformer Neural Network. * Nice to have experience with satellite image analysis, geospatial data analysis or remote sensing technologies. * Nice to have knowledge of terrain classification techniques and object detection in geospatial imagery. * Strong problem\-solving skills and ability to work independently.

title: Design Engineer – Transportation (Urban/Light Rail Environment)
description: **Design Engineer – Transportation (Urban/Light Rail Environment)** =================================================================== Roughan O’Donovan is looking to recruit a design engineer with three to seven years’ relevant experience for a permanent position within our transportation team **Job type:**Permanent **Location**: Sandyford, D.18 **About the role** ------------------ You will contribute to the delivery of projects focused on enhancing and upgrading transport infrastructure, with a particular emphasis on the design and coordination of interfaces with light rail systems. You will work closely with multidisciplinary teams to develop practical, high\-quality design solutions in complex urban environments. While experience in light rail projects is desirable, it is not essential, and candidates with transport design experience and an interest in integrated transport schemes are encouraged to apply. **About our transportation team** --------------------------------- Our highly skilled team has extensive experience in delivering transportation projects across Ireland and the UK. These include upgraded rail lines, light rail schemes, motorways, public transport infrastructure, rural roads and active travel projects. Our team works across the full lifecycle of transport infrastructure, from initial investigations and modelling through to detailed design, construction management and asset maintenance. Current and recent projects include: * TII Luas Construction Framework * Liffey Tolka Project * George’s Dock Bridge, Dublin * Grow College Green * BusConnects Dublin * enhancing Motorway Operations Services **Essential** ------------- * A bachelor’s degree in civil engineering (Level 8 in the National Framework of Qualifications) or equivalent * At least three years’ design or construction experience in civil engineering projects * Knowledge of relevant Irish and UK design standards (TII Publications, DMRB, DMURS, Traffic Signs, Cycle Design and Traffic Management Guidelines) * Experience in preparing reports * Experience of working with quality management systems * Proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Outlook) * Knowledge of BIM environments and associated workflows * Ability to work in a team environment * Motivated individual with a strong work ethic * Fluency/high level of proficiency in spoken and written English **Desirable** ------------- * A master’s degree in civil engineering (Level 9 in the National Framework of Qualifications) or equivalent * Chartered or working towards becoming a chartered member of a recognised professional body in the engineering sector * Competency in AutoCAD * Competency in 3D modelling software (Civil 3D, OpenRoads, MX) * Knowledge of traffic modelling software packages, such as Paramics, LinSig and Junctions * Experience in drainage design * Experience in pavement design * Experience in light rail projects **About Roughan O'Donovan** --------------------------- Roughan and O’Donovan (ROD) is a leading civil, environmental and structural engineering consultancy. An independently owned firm, we employ a multidisciplinary team of more than 260 people, of 20 different nationalities, in our Irish and UK offices. Our people are the heart of our business, and we invest heavily in their personal and professional development through all stages of career development. We are an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited Employer and operate an Institution of Civil Engineers (ICE) training scheme. We have well\-established graduate and chartership support programmes. We offer **wellness packages, flexible working and life\-event supports**, so whatever challenges life may bring, you can feel secure in the knowledge that you will be taken care of. **How to apply** ---------------- * Address your cover letter to Human Resources * Apply by clicking the link: https://shorturl.at/yoZTi Roughan O’Donovan is an equal opportunity employer. Roughan O'Donovan will retain, for at least one year, all records arising through the recruitment process for compliance purposes in line with the Employment Equality Acts, 1998\-2015\. Roughan O'Donovan is not accepting unsolicited CVs from search firms. CVs submitted by search firms without a valid written search agreement, including those submitted to hiring managers, are deemed to be the sole property of Roughan O'Donovan, and no fee will be paid in the event of candidate(s) being hired.

title: DevOps Engineer | €65k
description: Most DevOps roles are about operating systems that already exist. This one is about building the infrastructure that doesn’t exist yet. It's powering AI\-driven drug discovery through large\-scale simulation and computational science. We’re looking for a **hands\-on Platform Engineer who builds systems from the ground up** , not someone who configures tools built by others. **What you’ll do** * Build containerised systems using **Docker / Podman** * Design multi\-service environments using **Docker Compose / Swarm** * Build CI/CD pipelines end\-to\-end ( **GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins** ) * Develop automation and internal tooling used by engineering and R\&D teams * Work at Linux level (systemd, networking, shell scripting, debugging) * Support compute\-heavy scientific and AI workloads **Mandatory requirements** * Proven experience **building with Docker (not just running containers)** * Experience **designing and building CI/CD pipelines from scratch** * Strong **Linux fundamentals (shell scripting, networking, systemd)** * Hands\-on ability to build and structure infrastructure systems independently **Nice to have** * Python for automation and tooling * Observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, alerting systems) * Exposure to HPC, AI, simulation, or research computing environments * Infrastructure\-as\-Code (Ansible or similar) **You are not a fit if you:** * Only use managed DevOps/cloud platforms * Mainly configure existing pipelines and infrastructure * Don’t have strong Linux\-level troubleshooting ability * Prefer ticket\-based or maintenance\-style work **Why this role** This is not a DevOps maintenance role. It’s a **builder role inside a deep\-tech environment where infrastructure directly enables scientific discovery and AI\-driven drug development** . If you like designing systems properly from scratch, this will feel like home. APPLY NOW!

title: EMEA Digital Workspace Engineer
description: * we will consider applications from European locations where MetLife has a presence. Warsaw, Poland would be the preference. Our company is currently undergoing a significant transformation across the EMEA region. We operate six regional data centers that play a key role in supporting our test environments. Historically, each region has managed its own processes and infrastructure independently. However, we are now moving toward a more unified, global operating model. The goal of this transformation is to strengthen our internal knowledge base and improve collaboration between regional and global teams. By consolidating expertise and standardizing our practices, we aim to increase efficiency and reduce duplication of effort. This shift will also allow us to provide more consistent and scalable support worldwide. This transformation also focuses on improving collaboration with our internal business partners and responding more effectively to their needs. The Team You Will Join The Digital Workspace team, responsible for the desktop experience for users across the region, is part of an infrastructure team focused on transforming and supporting the technology platforms providing critical services to our businesses in around 20 countries across EMEA. The Opportunity In this critical role as an EMEA Digital Workspace Engineer, you will be responsible for ensuring the health and performance of all end\-user devices across EMEA, as well as the effective delivery of services and applications to the same. The ideal candidate has strong technical expertise in modern endpoint management tools and a passion for delivering an exceptional technology experience for our colleagues. You will be part of an extended global team and will collaborate with global functions on a daily basis. As an extended team with responsibility for Desktop Experience, Desktop Services, Desktop Application Services and PC Lifecycle you will coordinate and collaborate with these teams to ensure effective delivery of services to our region. How You’ll Help Us Build a Confident Future (Key Responsibilities) * Utilise advanced tools like Nexthink, and Microsoft Intune to monitor, maintain, and report on the performance and compliance of thousands of end\-user devices. * Develop and produce effective regular reporting, help define and monitor KPIs and performance trends. * Collaborate closely with global teams to manage endpoint builds, laptop currency and refresh cycles, and the seamless implementation of new user\-facing tools and features. * Champion the adoption and optimisation of new technologies, including AI tools like Microsoft Copilot for the desktop, to enhance user productivity. * Perform in\-depth issue identification and trend analysis on endpoint performance data to proactively identify problems and drive optimisation efforts. * Serve as a key technical contact point for desktop technology services within the EMEA region. What You Need to Succeed (Required Qualifications) * 2\+ years of experience in a similar digital workspace, desktop engineering, or endpoint management role. * Proven experience working and collaborating effectively within an extended technical team. * Good level of proficiency with modern endpoint management and monitoring tools, specifically Nexthink, Intune and Ivanti. * Good awareness of Microsoft endpoint management solutions generally (SCCM, Autopilot etc). * Excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with global teams. * Fluent and effective at communicating in English. * Bachelor’s degree in information technology, computer science, or equivalent practical experience. What Can Give You an Edge (Additional Skills) * Experience working within a large, global financial services or insurance company. * Hands\-on experience with the creation of Power\-BI dashboards to display key historical and future indicator information related to packaging, OS, app deployment and currency saturation. * Relevant industry certifications (e.g., Microsoft 365 Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate, ITIL Foundation, Nexthink or other relevant certifications). * Experience scripting with PowerShell for automation and reporting. **Location Expectation:** This is a hybrid role requiring a minimum of 3 days per week in office. Benefits We Offer Our Ireland benefits address holistic well\-being with programs for physical and mental health, financial wellness, and support for families. We are committed to employee care by delivering comprehensive inclusive offerings and cultivating an environment that drives professional growth, purposeful work, and a supportive culture. Our benefits package includes hybrid work, career development and professional qualification support, full private health insurance for employees and their immediate family members, income protection, defined contribution pension benefits and much more! About MetLife Recognized on Fortune magazine's list of the "World's Most Admired Companies" and Fortune World’s 25 Best Workplaces™, MetLife, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, is one of the world’s leading financial services companies; providing insurance, annuities, employee benefits and asset management to individual and institutional customers. With operations in more than 40 markets, we hold leading positions in the United States, Latin America, Asia, Europe, and the Middle East. Our purpose is simple \- to help our colleagues, customers, communities, and the world at large create a more confident future. United by purpose and guided by our core values \- Win Together, Do the Right Thing, Deliver Impact Over Activity, and Think Ahead \- we’re inspired to transform the next century in financial services. At MetLife, it’s \[1] \#AllTogetherPossible. Join us! References Visible links 1\. https://www.metlifecareers.com/en\_US/ml/GreatPlaceToWork

title: EMEA Regulatory Consultant
description: **Are you ready to embark on a career that truly affects people around the world? Trulioo invites you to be a catalyst for change in the dynamic realm of digital identity verification. As the global front\-runner in our industry, we are redefining how businesses grow, innovate and comply online.** Picture yourself at the forefront of innovation, contributing to our award\-winning platform that enables organizations worldwide to quickly onboard customers, optimize costs and combat fraud. Fueled by Silicon Valley support, Trulioo stands as the trusted platform that can verify more than 5 billion people and 700 million business entities spanning 195 countries. But Trulioo is more than a tech company. We are a united force of dedicated experts committed to establishing trust online \- and we’re proud to be recognized as a **BC Top Employer for the second consecutive year** , reflecting our commitment to an inclusive, collaborative, and people\-first workplace. Headquartered in Vancouver and with strategic hubs in San Diego and Dublin, we foster a culture of collaboration and open communication. Our offices support a hybrid model and staff typically work three days per week at a hub location. Join us where excitement meets innovation and contribute to a world where trust and technology unite. **What We Offer** * Comprehensive Benefits: We provide a robust benefits package for full\-time, permanent employees, including health, dental, and vision coverage, retirement plans with company match, paid time off, parental leave, and an annual education \& training stipend (equivalent to $1,000 in local currency). Specific benefits may vary by location and will be discussed further during the interview process. * Flexible Hybrid Working Environment: Our offices are designed to support both collaboration and flexibility. Enjoy weekly lunches, quality coffee, and regular social events. Many locations also feature parent rooms, on\-site gyms, comfortable lounges, and adaptable workstations to support your comfort and productivity. * Wellness: We care about your well\-being. Team members have access to wellness workshops and events, as well as a complimentary Headspace subscription to help you stay focused, grounded, and energized. * Employee Resource Groups: Belonging is an important part of doing your best work. Our ERGs provide an inclusive space, support and community for employees of diverse backgrounds and allies. We host informative, fun sessions and celebrations that are often open to the entire organization. **Position Summary** Trulioo is seeking an experienced EMEA Regulatory Consultant based in Dublin to serve as a subject matter expert on EU and UK regulatory frameworks impacting financial services, fintech, payments, marketplaces, crypto, and other regulated businesses. This role will provide deep expertise in Anti\-Money Laundering (AML), Counter\-Terrorist Financing (CTF), KYC, KYB, and related financial crimes regulations, as well as a strong working knowledge of PSD2 and payments regulation. The EMEA Regulatory Consultant will bridge regulatory expectations, compliance operations, and product development to ensure Trulioo designs and delivers compliant, market\-leading solutions for customers operating across the EU and UK. This individual will possess strong compliance operations experience, a comprehensive understanding of the evolving regulatory landscape, and the ability to critically analyze and interpret new and existing regulations impacting Trulioo’s customers. The ideal candidate will also have exposure to the Digital Services Act (DSA), Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA), Identity and eIDAS regulations as they relate to digital identity, trust services, and platform regulation. Experience with consuming, analyzing, and distilling EU and UK regulatory developments is essential. This will be a full\-time, permanent position, preferably working out of the Dublin office on a hybrid model (3 days per week in the office). However, this role could be done remotely for the right candidate. **What You'll Be Doing** **Financial Crimes \& Payments Regulatory Expertise** * Provide subject matter expertise on EU and UK AML/CTF, KYC, KYB, sanctions, and PEP screening regulations. * Maintain strong knowledge of PSD2 and broader EU/UK payments regulatory frameworks. * Monitor evolving legislative and supervisory developments across EMEA jurisdictions. * Analyze and interpret regulatory changes and translate them into actionable internal and product guidance. * Support regulatory engagement, customer due diligence requests, and audit processes as needed. **Product Strategy \& Development Partnership** * Act as a key regulatory SME to Product and Engineering teams during design, build, and UAT phases. * Ensure Trulioo’s products align with EU/UK regulatory expectations and address our customers' operational needs efficiently and effectively. * Provide insight into financial crime typologies, payment compliance practices, and emerging supervisory trends. * Support commercialization by validating regulatory positioning of new and existing solutions. **Compliance Operations \& Industry Practice** * Bring hands\-on compliance operations experience, including onboarding, transaction monitoring, investigations, SAR/STR reporting, and regulatory examinations. * Advise on operational best practices used by regulated entities in marketplaces, fintech, payments, financial services, and crypto sectors. * Identify operational challenges and translate them into product enhancements and advisory insights. **Regulatory Research, Analysis \& Thought Leadership** * Conduct structured regulatory research across the EU and UK frameworks affecting Trulioo’s customers. * Distill complex regulatory developments into clear internal briefings and executive\-level summaries. * Influence Trulioo’s strategy based on key regulatory developments trends and identify opportunities to create/enhance products to address our customers' emerging regulatory challenges * Partner with Marketing and Executive teams to create thought leadership content. * Represent Trulioo at EMEA conferences, panels, and industry forums. **Compliance Management System (CMS) \& Culture** * Support the continued development and maturity of Trulioo’s Compliance Management System within EMEA. * Help design scalable governance processes aligned with licensing and regulatory obligations. * Embed a culture of compliance that balances regulatory rigor with innovation. * Ensure internal frameworks evolve alongside Trulioo’s growth in the region. **What You’ll Bring** * 8–15\+ years of experience in AML, financial crimes compliance, payments regulation, or regulatory advisory roles within EU/UK markets. * Strong expertise in EU AML Directives, UK AML regime, sanctions frameworks, and PSD2\. * Hands\-on compliance operations experience within regulated financial institutions, fintech, or crypto environments. * Demonstrated ability to research, evaluate, and critically analyze regulatory developments across multiple jurisdictions. * Experience in distilling complex regulations into practical operational and product guidance. * Strong communication skills and ability to influence cross\-functional teams and senior leadership. * Experience supporting regulatory examinations or supervisory engagements is highly desirable. **Nice To Have** * Exposure to DSA, DORA, eIDAS, digital identity, or trust services regulation. * Advanced certifications such as CAMS or equivalent financial crimes credentials. * Experience working with RegTech or compliance technology providers. * Demonstrated experience speaking at industry events or publishing regulatory insights. **Thriving at Trulioo** At Trulioo, you’re not just an employee. You’re a valued member of our Trulicrew on a journey of professional and personal growth with a world\-class organization. With Trulioo, you have the power to revolutionize the intersection of technology, digital trust and online identity to open the global economy to everyone. Together, we can shape the digital future. We’re on the lookout for exceptional people to empower with trust, autonomy and the freedom to cultivate their potential. Your curiosity, meticulous attention to detail and passion to contribute are highly valued. If that resonates with you, apply today to become a part of our team. Join us in shaping a future where your career isn’t just a journey but also a boundless exploration into the possibilities of technology and digital identity verification. Get an inside look at life at Trulioo by following us on LinkedIn, or learn more about us at www.trulioo.com *If you don’t see yourself fully reflected in every job requirement listed on the posting above, we still encourage you to reach out and apply. Research has shown that minorities and underrepresented groups often only apply when they feel 100% qualified. We are committed to creating a more equitable, inclusive and diverse company and we strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and life experiences to apply.* *Trulioo welcomes applications from people with disabilities. Support is available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. Finally, we know from time\-to\-time emergencies happen and you may need to reschedule an interview \- we understand and encourage you to be in communication without worrying about losing the opportunity or your credibility.* **Privacy Notice** As part of our job application process, Trulioo collects, processes, and discloses personal data for the purpose of identifying suitable candidates for our job openings. For more detail, please visit Trulioo’s Website Privacy Policy in the section “When you apply for a job”.
