You’ll take ownership of projects from tender through to final account, and you’ll have the commercial influence to actually make a dent in profitability — not just report on it afterwards. This isn’t a role where you sit in the background issuing spreadsheets into the void. You’ll be partnering closely with Project Managers, Engineers, subcontractors and clients, making sure the commercial heartbeat of each project stays healthy and predictable (or at least explainable). **What You’ll Be Doing (the Short Version)** * Building and managing BOQs, budgets, reports and profit plans without needing someone hovering over you. * Running interim commercial meetings with clients and internal teams — and being the person who knows exactly where the project stands commercially. * Managing subcontractor accounts, valuations and final accounts, ensuring payments follow agreed terms and retentions don’t fall through the cracks. * Issuing accurate monthly applications, including remeasures, dayworks, changes and any contract claims worth fighting for. * Spotting commercial risks early, raising them quickly and helping to keep surprises to a minimum. * Working closely with Planning Engineers to keep progress reporting aligned with reality rather than optimism. * Costing and agreeing daily work changes, including remeasurement of subcontractor work and site variations. * Keeping meticulous records — because commercial claims don’t win themselves. * Negotiating with clients or their representatives when needed. * Setting a standard of enthusiasm, accuracy and reliability that others can follow. * Keeping information secure and complying with the company’s Information Security Management System. **Who You Probably Are** * Someone who combines accuracy with speed — not one or the other. * Someone who can prioritise their own workload without waiting to be told what’s urgent. * Someone who knows the commercial implications of scheduling decisions and isn’t shy about saying so. * Someone who understands that good communication prevents expensive misunderstandings. **Why this role matters:** The Electrical QS has more influence on project outcomes than most people realise. When you do your job well, the company delivers better margins, fewer disputes and cleaner final accounts. When you don’t, things get messy. We’re hiring someone who takes pride in keeping things un‑messy. *For more information on this role, or other Construction / Engineering opportunities across Ireland, the UK, Europe, or South Africa please review our careers page (Vacancies – Kirby Group Careers) or contact the Talent Acquisition Team at Kirby Group in confidence on \+353 (0\)1 454 0411\.* *Kirby Group Engineering is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, regardless of race, gender, disability, religion, sexual orientation, or age.*

title: Electrical Technician
description: **JLL empowers you to shape a brighter way**. Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology for our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people and empowering them to thrive, grow meaningful careers and to find a place where they belong. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades or technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, join our team as we help shape a brighter way forward. **Electrical Technician** ------------------------- **Role Overview** ----------------- We are seeking a qualified Electrical Technician to join our facilities management team in Ireland. The successful candidate will be responsible for the installation, maintenance, repair, and testing of electrical systems and equipment across commercial properties, ensuring compliance with Irish electrical regulations and safety standards. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ The Electrical Technician will carry out planned preventative maintenance and reactive repairs on electrical systems including lighting, power distribution, emergency lighting, fire alarms, and building management systems. You will respond to electrical faults and breakdowns, diagnose problems efficiently, and implement effective solutions to minimize downtime. Conducting electrical safety testing and inspections in accordance with ETCI National Rules for Electrical Installations and maintaining accurate records of all work completed form essential parts of the role. You will ensure all electrical work complies with relevant Irish legislation, health and safety requirements, and building codes. Collaborating with other trades and contractors on site, you will support project work and minor installations as required. The role includes maintaining electrical equipment inventories, ordering parts and materials, and providing technical guidance to junior staff members when needed. **Required Qualifications and Experience** ------------------------------------------ * Candidates must hold a recognized electrical qualification such as Advanced Certificate Craft \- Electrical or equivalent Level 6 qualification on the NFQ. Current Safe Electric Registration (RECI or ECSSA) is essential. * A minimum of 3\-5 years' post\-qualification experience in commercial or facilities maintenance environments is required, along with comprehensive knowledge of Irish electrical regulations, ETCI wiring rules, and health and safety legislation. * Strong diagnostic and problem\-solving abilities are necessary, with the ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Excellent communication skills and customer service orientation are important, as is proficiency in using computerized maintenance management systems **Desirable Skills** -------------------- * Experience with building management systems (BMS), HVAC controls, and access control systems would be advantageous. * Knowledge of energy management systems and sustainability initiatives is beneficial. * Previous experience in corporate office environments or large commercial properties is valued, as are relevant health and safety certifications including manual handling and working at heights. **What We Offer** ----------------- We provide competitive salary packages commensurate with experience, along with comprehensive benefits including pension schemes, health insurance options, and ongoing professional development opportunities. You will work within a supportive team environment with opportunities for career progression and exposure to diverse commercial real estate projects across Ireland. **Location:** On\-site –Dublin, IRL If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table! At JLL, we harness the power of artificial intelligence (AI) to efficiently accelerate meaningful connections between candidates and opportunities. Using AI capabilities, we analyze your application for relevant skills, experiences, and qualifications to generate valuable insights about how your unique profile aligns with the specific requirements of the role you're pursuing. ***JLL Privacy Notice*** Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL), together with its subsidiaries and affiliates, is a leading global provider of real estate and investment management services. We take our responsibility to protect the personal information provided to us seriously. Generally the personal information we collect from you are for the purposes of processing in connection with JLL’s recruitment process. We endeavour to keep your personal information secure with appropriate level of security and keep for as long as we need it for legitimate business or legal reasons. We will then delete it safely and securely. For more information about how JLL processes your personal data, please view our Candidate Privacy Statement. For additional details please see our career site pages for each country. Jones Lang LaSalle (“JLL”) is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the employment process – including the online application and/or overall selection process – you may email us at HRSCLeaves@jll.com. This email is only to request an accommodation. Please direct any other general recruiting inquiries to our Contact Us page \> I want to work for JLL.

title: Electrical Technician Pharma
description: **Duration: Permanent** **Location: Dublin West** **Working Hours: Day role** This position will be based on a biopharmaceutical site on behalf of our client, a multinational and multicultural company which provides great career progression opportunities to the right person. **Purpose of the role:** We are seeking a highly motivated and safety conscious individual with a mechanical or electrical background to join the critical utilities hard services maintenance and calibrations contract for one of the world’s leading pharmaceutical companies based in Dublin. The Electrical Shift Technician is responsible for ensuring that all utilities equipment in his/her charge for the duration of shift is always available, and all alarms and deviations are responded to and resolved or litigated as efficiently as possible. **Daily Tasks:** * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and the company’s EHS rules and guidelines. * Operation and monitoring of site critical utilities systems. * First line and scheduled maintenance for utilities equipment. * In conjunction with shift team and shift lead, ensure all equipment deviations are responded to, reported and escalated as per site procedures in the first instance. * Liaise with subcontractors during planned service maintenance. * Ensure that all work permits are reviewed and signed as area owner by the relevant shift members. * Completion of all related documentation in compliance with site and regulatory rules and guidelines. * Delivery of delegated engineering tasks in compliance with good engineering practice and all regulatory, statutory and corporate requirements. * Assist engineering support team in the update of Utilities Standard Operating Procedures and ensure training is carried out with Operations Teams. * Be fully aware of the terms of the site IPPC (Integrated Pollution Prevention Control) licence and ensure that all actions/operations on site are within the terms. * Ensure training curricula is kept up to date with client requirements, i.e. SOP’s (Standard Operating Procedures), EWI’s (Engineering Work Instructions), etc. * Carryout Investigations on utilities equipment, identify problem areas and assist in improvement procedures. * Liaise closely with the site utilities Engineering Support Team. * In conjunction with shift team and shift lead, ensure all issues over shift are communicated to the oncoming shift in a smooth and efficient manner. * Assist maintenance departments with planned and unplanned shutdowns. * Professional and courteous interaction with all personnel on site. * Update stores spare lists as appropriate. * Participate in utilities reliability programs. Participate in local reviews of maintenance performance data using published maintenance excellence KPI results, areas that will be reviewed and improvements sought: * High levels of paperwork * PM:CM ratio * Maintenance man\-hours per equipment/system * MTBF * MTTR * Equipment reliability trends * Any other activities as directed by the Contract Manager or Shift Lead. **Qualifications and skills:** * Senior Trades, City \& Guilds or equivalent in electrical engineering. * Minimum 2 years’ experience post qualification in industrial utilities, preferably in pharmaceuticals. * Good Initiative and Pro\-active attitude in approach to duties. * Must be able to organize and prioritize tasks; must be detail orientated and self\-motivated. * Must have excellent written and verbal communication skills as well as the ability to accurately document work performed. **Must have:** * Maintenance and operational experience of pumps, HVAC Systems, Compressors, Boilers, steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, CIP systems, Low /Medium voltage Electrical Systems * Building Management Systems (BMC/QBMS/MCS/ECS/EMS) * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance Systems. * Experience with and understanding PLC and SCADA control systems * Work within a Computerized Maintenance Management Work Order system environment. * Work within a documented Permit to Work System * Computer literate * Ability to read and understand P\&ID’s. * Understanding of SOP’s and GMP Engineering systems. * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation. Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Team Player. * Attention to detail * Methodical \& Analytical approach to work. * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability. **Good to have:** * Experience of working on a 24/7 production site. * Experience dealing with shift teams. * Ability to work in a team atmosphere, with the ability to recognize and * facilitate conflicts through to resolution. * Possess the highest level of attention to detail and expect this of others. * Effectively communicate with all levels within the company and client team as necessary. * Approach problem solving in a logical and systematic way. * Have the courage of conviction to make decisions when required.

title: Electrical Utilities Technician Pharma
description: **Location: Dublin 22** **Duration: Permanent** **Hours: 39 per week (day role)** This position will be based on a biopharmaceutical site on behalf of our client, a multinational and multicultural company which provides great career progression opportunities to the right person. **Overview:** The Electrical Utilities Technician is responsible for the execution of the preventive and corrective maintenance program as per the Client/PPU requirements. He/she will work as part of the Maintenance and Calibration team on a major client Biopharmaceutical site. He/she will liaise with the contract team, client engineering and equipment vendors to ensure maximum uptime of all utilities, plant and equipment, and that equipment is in compliance with all regulatory, statutory and client corporate requirements. **Duties include:** * Carry out work as per schedule supplied by the Maintenance Coordinator, Planner and as per site policies, schedules and procedures. * Troubleshoot problems with utilities equipment as necessary and as directed by site supervisors. * Complete site documentation before and after work tasks, as appropriate. * Flexibility to work on equipment in all buildings. * Work and interact with the site DES team. * Flexibility to work around Maintenance Shutdown’s. * Be familiar with all GMP rules and regulations. * Be familiar with Statutory requirements. * Liaise and work with the Shift/Day Maintenance Team on all Utilities Equipment. * Liaise with Contractors during planned service maintenance. * Participate in the appropriate training. * Carry out Risk Assessment of Equipment Maintenance Task List steps and ensure these are always kept valid. * Update MTL’s as appropriate. * Carry out Periodic reviews of EWI/SOP to ensure documents are always current and valid. * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and company EHS rules and guidelines. * Be responsible for ITP and ensure missing requirements are complete. * Participate in Audits of the Mechanical rooms and follow up on actions. * Ensure all tools and equipment are kept in good condition. * Day\-to\-day relationship with company’s Contract Manager and contract team. * Day\-to\-day relationship with client Engineering manager and representatives. * Manage Technical investigations for utility operations group and implement changes that occur from these investigations. * Complete Data Build on new equipment (create maintenance plans, preparing BOM, SOP’s, advising on rounds and readings for operation). * Evaluate Electrical PM job plan content and frequency. Recommend and implement improvements to optimise utility reliability, availability, and compliance. * Continuously evaluate equipment maintenance and failure history. Determine appropriateness of PM content and frequency. * Execute PM program along with the rest of the team. * Manage a program to measure and improve the effectiveness of the electrical PM program. Establish and evaluate effectiveness metrics to identify areas for improvement. * Supporting site contract maintenance team in minimizing downtime and risk of downtime to facility * Trouble\-shooting and investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures along with the Shift Operation Group, this includes the Fire Alarm Detection system. * Introduction and installation of Reliability Centred Maintenance on all existing and future equipment. * Updating of utilities related standard operating procedures. * Identifying, liaising with, and, where appropriate, co\-ordination of third party contractors and suppliers on site. * Liaising with site projects team as clients engineering representative. * Liaising with site contract team to identify suppliers and quantities of spare parts; review and updating of spare part inventory of spares in clients site store. * Liaising with contract team and client to review maintenance programme, and effecting changes to methods and procedures where appropriate. * Continuous improvement of contract delivery on site. * Coordinating with personnel on utility requirements upon purchase of new equipment, movement of equipment, technology transfer etc. * Identifying and implementing, where appropriate, energy saving schemes. * Liaise with Quality and external auditing bodies upon audits. * Ensuring Engineering areas, in liaison with Contract Manager and Contract co\-ordinator, facilities and equipment are kept in a clean, safe and serviceable condition, and general housekeeping high standards are maintained. * Manage the execution of the utilities maintenance program and organisation structure in order to achieve and sustain optimum reliability, maintainability, useful life, and life cycle cost for utilities assets. * Implement and participate in utilities reliability programs as per the clients Global Reliability Program. * Drive local reviews of maintenance performance data using published maintenance excellence KPI results, areas that will be reviewed and improvements sought: \- Spares cost \- High levels of paperwork \- PM:CM ratio \- Maintenance man\-hours per equipment/system \- MTBF \- MTTR \- Equipment reliability trends * PM program content is to be reviewed on regular basis to remove non\-value added activities’ and duplication. * Develop long term (5 year) life cycle costing for asset replacement plans (equipment moving towards end of life). * Ensure that the CMMS system contains the most up to date information regarding equipment data, spares and maintenance man\-hours. * Maximise maintenance wrench time utilisation through effective planning. **Required skills:** * Electrical experience, 2 years minimum and good understanding of the basic fundamentals of the various utilities systems. * Pumps, HVAC Systems, Compressors, Boilers, steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, CIP systems, Low /Medium voltage Electrical Systems. * Building Management Systems (Scada). * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance Systems. * PLC’s. * Work within a Computerized Maintenance Management Work Order system environment. * Experience with Facility Start Up. * Work within a documented Permit to Work System. * Computer literate. * Ability to read and understand Electrical Drawings. * Understanding of SOP’s and GMP Engineering systems. * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation. * Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Troubleshooting / repair of Low \& medium Voltage Electrical Systems * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability of motors/VFD’s, panels and Electrical safety systems * Vast knowledge of single and three phase systems (Motors) * Knowledge of Mechanical systems * Team Player

title: Electrician (Night Shift)
description: **About The Role** The Role We are seeking an experienced **Industrial Electrician** to support the installation, maintenance, and fault\-finding of electrical systems across our operational sites. You will work on production\-critical machinery and infrastructure, supporting continuous operations while ensuring the highest safety and compliance standards are maintained. Key Responsibilities * Install, commission, and align electrical systems, machinery, and equipment * Carry out planned preventative maintenance and reactive electrical repairs * Diagnose and rectify electrical faults to minimise downtime * Work on industrial production equipment, including motors, VSDs, PLC\-controlled systems, contactors, and instrumentation * Support mechanical breakdowns where required and work collaboratively with mechanical teams * Ensure all work complies with electrical safety standards, site procedures, and legislation * Maintain accurate records of maintenance, repairs, and inspections * Handle materials, components, and tools safely and efficiently * Work closely with Engineers, Supervisors, and Site Management to improve reliability and performance * Participate in a weekend on\-call rota as required What We’re Looking For * FETAC Level 6 qualification or equivalent in Electrical Engineering / Electrical Trade * Minimum 2 years’ post\-apprenticeship experience * National Trade Certificate / Trade Card * Proven experience working on industrial sites or production environments * Strong technical knowledge of: + Motors, contactors, VSDs + PLC\-controlled systems + Industrial wiring, containment, instrumentation, starters, and temporary installations * An interest in mechanical systems and the ability to support mechanical breakdowns * MEWP experience is advantageous (training can be provided) * Full driving licence and own transport advantageous * Fluent English * Flexible and willing to participate in a weekend shift rota Key Skills \& Behaviours * Strong problem\-solving and fault\-finding capability * Calm and focused under pressure in live production environments * Able to work independently and as part of a wider engineering team * Well\-organised with good communication and reporting skills * Strong safety mindset and attention to detail What We Offer * Competitive salary (DOE) * Company vehicle provided * 20 days annual leave * Full training and ongoing development support * PPE and all required safety equipment provided * Clear career progression opportunities within Panda’s engineering and operations teams * Long\-term stability within a growing, future\-focused organisation **About You** About Us **We are Panda. We value waste.** We’re in the business of waste management but that doesn’t just mean collecting bins. Behind every collection is a passionate team of people finding smarter, safer, and more sustainable ways to recycle and repurpose materials that would otherwise go to waste. Over the past 30 years, Panda has grown into a leading resource recovery business, bringing together almost 3,000 people across multiple brands, united by a shared vision and strong values. While our operations are diverse, our purpose is clear: to protect our people, support our customers, and create positive impact for the communities and environments we serve. At Panda, **people are at the heart of everything we do** . We work as one team, combining different skills, experiences, and perspectives to achieve shared goals. We care for each other’s wellbeing, encourage development, and support growth because we know we’re stronger together. We expect **excellence** in how we operate. High standards, bold thinking, and continuous improvement drive our passion for innovation. We challenge the ordinary, put safety first, and embrace new ideas while delivering practical, cost\-effective solutions that move our business forward. We **own what we do** . From day\-to\-day tasks to major projects, we take pride in our work and responsibility for the outcome. We don’t walk past problems we address them together. Our expertise, reliability, and care reflect who we are and the difference we make. And above all, we act with **trust and integrity** . Trust is built through open communication, honesty, and accountability. It allows us to move quickly, make confident decisions, and deliver results — knowing our people are empowered to do the right thing. Our journey depends on talented, committed people who want to make an impact. **Take the first step and join us on the journey.** *Panda is committed to attracting and retaining a skilled and diverse workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate and the people we serve. title: Enterprise Resources Planning Consultant
description: About the Role We are looking for ambitious, analytical, and customer\-focused individuals to join our growing team. This role is ideal for someone coming from an accounting, manufacturing, operations, customer service, supply chain, or business support background who enjoys solving problems, improving processes, and working with technology. You do not need to be a “Digital Transformation Consultant” already — we will provide full training and mentoring. What matters most is your attitude, communication skills, curiosity, and ability to learn. Experience with accounting software, business systems, manufacturing planning software, or ERP platforms would be a strong advantage, but it is not essential. About the Company We are a growing business technology consultancy helping companies modernise how they work through smarter systems, automation, reporting, AI tools, and process improvement. We work with businesses across multiple industries including manufacturing, distribution, finance, and professional services. Our environment is collaborative, fast\-paced, and focused on continuous learning. We believe technology should make businesses simpler, faster, and more efficient — not more complicated. What You’ll Be Doing * Work closely with clients to understand their business processes and operational challenges * Support the implementation and improvement of business and accounting software systems * Help customers improve reporting, workflows, and operational efficiency * Provide system support, troubleshooting, and user guidance * Analyse data and identify opportunities for process improvement * Use Microsoft Excel and AI tools to improve productivity and problem\-solving * Participate in client meetings, workshops, training sessions, and project activities * Continuously learn new technologies and business systems Who Would Suit This Role? This role would suit someone with 2–5 years of experience in areas such as: * Accounting or finance * Manufacturing or production planning * Operations or administration * Customer support or business support * Business software support * Supply chain or logistics * Systems coordination or reporting roles Skills \& Qualities We’re Looking For \- Essential * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Customer\-focused mindset * Comfortable learning new software and technologies * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Excel * Proactive attitude and willingness to learn * Comfortable working in a hybrid environment Advantageous (but not essential) * Experience with accounting software or business systems * Exposure to manufacturing planning or operational software * Experience supporting users or customers * Interest in AI and digital tools * Experience working with process improvement initiatives Requirements * Must have valid permission to work in Ireland * Full driving license preferred * Candidates with a learner permit may also be considered if they can obtain a full licence within 6 months Benefits: * Company events * Work from home Licence/Certification: * full or provisional drivers licence (preferred)

title: Entrepreneurship Instructor (AI Training)
description: **About The Role** We're looking for experienced entrepreneurship educators and business experts to help evaluate and improve AI systems trained on startups, innovation, and business planning. Your real\-world knowledge will directly shape how AI understands and communicates the principles of building and scaling businesses — making a meaningful impact on the next generation of entrepreneurship tools. * Organization: Alignerr (Powered by Labelbox) * Type: Hourly / Task\-Based Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Review AI\-generated startup scenarios, business plans, and entrepreneurship content for accuracy and practical relevance * Assess the quality of business models, financial assumptions, and go\-to\-market strategies * Identify unrealistic projections, flawed reasoning, or gaps in early\-stage decision\-making logic * Provide structured, actionable feedback to help AI produce more reliable and grounded business guidance * Work independently and asynchronously on your own schedule **Who You Are** * 3\+ years of experience teaching entrepreneurship, small business, or startup strategy * Strong understanding of business models, venture stages, and early\-stage decision\-making * Able to critically evaluate written business plans and identify what's missing or misleading * Comfortable delivering clear, applied, real\-world feedback at scale * Self\-motivated and reliable in an asynchronous remote environment **Nice to Have** * MBA or entrepreneurship\-focused graduate degree * Experience as a founder, startup advisor, or angel investor * Familiarity with AI tools, content evaluation workflows, or edtech platforms **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects with top research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work on your own schedule * Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration * Contribute to meaningful work that improves how AI understands business and entrepreneurship * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

title: Equity Analyst
description: **At Mako, we believe in the power of collaboration to drive innovation in pursuit of our collective ambition; excellence in trading. Our diverse community is connected through a commitment to being the best we can be with the highest standards of integrity.** Join the equity delta one team as an Equity Analyst. You will work directly with experienced senior traders and analysts to help them identify risks and opportunities in equities markets that help deliver sustainable short and long\-term success. The Equity Analyst will work closely with trading teams to create and manage research that is relevant, accurate and actionable. **What you'll be doing:** * Building and owning sector coverage across a focused company universe * Conducting fundamental research including catalyst identification, scenario modelling, and earnings analysis * Monitoring news, filings, and macro events for real\-time impact on covered names * Constructing and maintaining financial models and proprietary datasets * Developing intuition for how information flows into price — understanding what the market is pricing and why * Tracking structural trends — technological shifts, regulatory change, macro developments — and translating them into actionable equity views * Continuously improving research processes through practical, scalable solution **What we're looking for:** * Excellent analytical skills with very strong attention to detail * Clear, concise communicator who knows what matters and can cut through noise * Genuine intellectual curiosity about equities * Bachelor's degree or equivalent * Front office or markets\-adjacent experience (US equities a plus, not required) **We are Mako** **Benefits** At Mako, we are welcoming, inclusive and collaborative. We work fast and smart in a supportive and dress\-down environment that allows colleagues to be themselves and achieve great things. We uphold the principles of a flat structure that offers unrivalled engagement with senior leadership and career development opportunities. We have a comprehensive benefits package, including: * Flexible leave and hybrid working policies * Private health and dental insurance * Generous pension scheme * Free access to the Mako gym * Employee wellbeing guidance and support * Opportunity to become involved in the rewarding work of the Mako Foundation **About Mako** Mako is a leading options market maker with a global trading footprint. It has been at the forefront of options market making since 1999, from the open outcry trading pits to screen trading and automated algorithmic execution strategies that are driving the future of the industry. From offices in London, Dublin, Amsterdam, Singapore, Sydney, Brisbane and Chengdu, Mako offers the best\-in\-class liquidity solutions across Equities, Fixed Income, Commodities and FX derivatives markets and prides itself in its entrepreneurial, collaborative and philanthropic culture. If you require any reasonable adjustments or assistance during the recruitment process, please email hr@mako.com and we will arrange this. For further information on the Mako Group please refer to our website: www.mako.com. *Mako does not accept unsolicited CVs or candidate details from recruiters or search firms and will not pay any fees to such firms without a signed agreement.*

title: Events Bartender
description: Fallon \& Byrne is Dublin’s premier food destination and home to our bustling Foodhall, Wine Cellar, Dining Room and The Exchange. Fallon \& Byrne is renowned for its dedication to quality, exceptional customer experiences and building community through food. With over 160 employees, we are committed to fostering a supportive and vibrant workplace culture that reflects our values of excellence, empathy, innovation, integrity and authenticity. We are seeking enthusiastic Bartending Members to join our Event's team to support a variety of events, from weddings, to corporate events and parties. Please note that the majority of these events are held on weekends. **The Person** * Experience: A minimum of 1 year experience in hotels, events, or a restaurant environment. \- Experience with fine dining is a plus! * Proficiency in English Language essential. * A warm and friendly personality \- someone who genuinely enjoys working with people. * Impeccable attention to detail and a love for creating a polished and welcoming atmosphere. * Ensuring every guest has a memorable experience from start to finish of their event. * Great communication skills \- someone who can engage with both guests and the team in a positive manner. * A passion for providing excellent service, particularly in an event environment. * Working hours will vary depending on the event schedule, so flexibility is important \- particularly at weekends. * You’re passionate about what you do and bring your authentic self to work everyday * You’re innovative in your approach to your role and not afraid to think outside the box * You’re an empathetic leader, with the ability to inspire others * You combine analytical and methodical thinking with high attention to detail and a solution\-focused approach * You have a high standard of integrity, with sound judgement and a commitment to excellence * You are an excellent relationship builder and focus on building trust and representing Fallon \& Byrne **Working with Fallon \& Byrne** At Fallon \& Byrne we are committed to surpassing our customers expectations. By joining our team, you will be part of a supportive and inspiring environment designed to help you achieve your best work. We offer abundant opportunities and the support needed to build an exceptional career across our diverse business specialisms. **What we offer:** In return for your dedication, you will receive an excellent benefits package, including; * Hourly rate of €14\.15 * The opportunity to work with a growing organisation with a growth mindset and a strong, dynamic leadership team * The ability to move and grow within the business as it grows * Opportunities for professional development * A fun working environment with a great team around you * Wellness programme * Generous discounts on purchases in The Dining Room, Wine Cellar and Foodhall * Subsidised meals on shift * Company events and social gatherings Fallon \& Byrne is an equal opportunities employer. We believe that the values of our people set us apart. We celebrate diversity, champion equality and promote inclusion in our approach to everything we do. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, cultures, tastes and experiences. Your individuality is our strength.

title: Executive Officer
description: The Courts Service is currently seeking people to join their team as Court Registrars within the Family, Criminal, and Civil court offices. Being a Court Registrar offers a unique insight into the running of the Irish courts system. This role presents dynamic and fulfilling challenge where each day brings new experiences. The role of Court Registrar is key to the efficient running of the court and requires good organisation skills. As the official record keeper for the Court, this includes providing support pre\-court, in\-court and post\-court to the Judge and the parties. The Registrar is the key link between the Court Office and the Court. The Court Registrar is responsible for communication between all parties, feeding back on issues and working with their office team to ensure the administration of Justice. Teamwork is a key feature of this role. In this role, you will gain invaluable exposure to court practice and procedure, legislation, litigation, and decision making. In some offices Court Registrars will manage staff. This will include assigning tasks, probation, performance management and training and development. The experience gained as a Court Registrar is transferable and beneficial to a fulfilling and rewarding career within the Courts Service. The post will primarily be located in **Naas Court Office** and work is performed on site. There is no hybrid or remote working. The post will also require travel to Athy Court office on a roster basis. Key responsibilities information can found in the competition booklet through the advertisement link. **Essential Requirements:** On the closing date of 3rd July 2026 candidates **must** have: * Be at least 18 years of age on or before the closing date. * Fulfil the Citizenship requirements * Ensure that they meet the criteria regarding Public \& Civil Service Redundancy/III Health Retirement Schemes. * A Leaving Certificate or equivalent or * Be serving as a Civil Servant with at least two years’ service or * Have 3\+ years demonstrated experience in a customer facing role in an unrelated field or * Have 2\+ years in a related field or * Have 1\+ years experience working in the Courts Service. Relevant knowledge and skills to undertake the duties of the position, including a strong command of the English language and the ability to: * Take direction/follow instructions. * Organise and prioritise work effectively. * Work well with the public and colleagues. * Be flexible in their approach to work. * Be able to communicate effectively in a clear and concise manner. * Have a strong work ethic and willingness to learn. * Basic level and experience of relevant ICT skills **Starting Pay:** €38,803\.00 gross per year **APPLICATION PROCESS** * Application should be made by logging into the advertisement link. * The closing date is 12 noon 3rd July 2026 **.** * Applications received after the closing date and time will not be accepted. * Interviews will take place in person in Naas

title: Executive – IT (JD-0079)
description: **Job Description** The IT Executive will provide Level 1 (basic support) and where applicable, Level 2 (advanced troubleshooting) technical assistance to the hospital management and staff, ensuring smooth operation of hospital IT and communication systems, networks, peripherals devices and applications. This role is critical in maintaining very high uptime if infrastructure for clinical and administrative workflows, safeguarding patient data and supporting healthcare\-specific applications. The role will need good writing skills to develop IT processes and Flow charts. **Roles \& Responsibilities** – An ideal candidate would have the educational qualification and experience to support many of the following – * Helpdesk \& User Support * Primary point of contact for all IT\-related incidents and service requests. * Provide timely end\-user support across all hospital departments, including clinical, administrative, and operations. * Document issues, resolutions, and IT assets through a helpdesk or ticketing system. * Train hospital staff on basic IT usage, security awareness, and system protocols. * Hardware Support \& Maintenance * Provide L1/L2 desktop support including computers, laptops, printers, and peripherals. * Maintain hardware inventory, coordinate procurement, and manage vendors for IT assets. * Monitor and maintain the structured cabling system and network rack organization, and troubleshoot network\-related issues. * Support installation of new workstations, IT equipment, and departmental expansions in line with company IT and security policy. * Develop IT procedures as per IT policy. * Biometric / Access Control System * Install, configure, and administer biometric and access control devices. * Manage access control system data, device synchronization, and software updates. * Troubleshoot hardware and software issues and coordinate with the vendor as required. * CCTV System * Plan, install, and maintain IP\-based and analog CCTV surveillance systems across hospital premises. * Manage NVR/DVR recording systems, storage, and camera health monitoring. * Perform regular audits, maintenance, and troubleshooting of camera systems. * IP PBX System * Plan, install, and maintain IP\-based telephones and IP PBX systems across hospital premises. * Manage extensions, speed dials, and IP PBX health monitoring. * Perform regular audits, maintenance, and troubleshooting of systems. * BGM, Digital Signage / TV, and AV System * Install, configure, and maintain the systems. * Manage system data, device synchronization, and updates. * Troubleshoot hardware and software issues and coordinate with the vendor as required. * Provide day\-to\-day support to end users for AV solutions. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Required: Bachelor’s Degree / Diploma / ITI in IT related technical field. * 2–5 years of hands\-on experience in IT infrastructure, networking, and system support. * Ability to troubleshoot application issues and coordinate with software vendors. * Prior experience in a hospital, clinic, or healthcare IT environment is a strong advantage. * Familiarity with Hospital Management Software (HMS) / Hospital Information System (HIS) platforms desirable. **Remuneration:** As per industry standards **Why Join Us:** At Sant Nirankari Health City, we offer a collaborative and supportive work environment where your contributions are valued and recognized. Join us in our mission to deliver exceptional healthcare services while upholding the highest standards of quality and patient care. Apply Now ← Back to Careers Page

title: FX Forwards Product Sales Executive (Hungarian Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We’re looking for an FX Forwards Product Sales Executive to build and drive a sales pipeline for our range of FX derivatives. You'll bring your product expertise to the Sales team to help close complex sales opportunities by cross\-selling and pitching to our existing base of Revolut customers and prospects. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Working independently to build and close a customer pipeline * Prospecting directly across the existing base of Revolut Business customers by focusing on a solution\-based sales approach * Communicating Revolut's FX forward value proposition to prospective customers in Hungary by identifying issues with their current providers * Building confidence, skills, and expertise in FX derivatives across the Sales team * Sharing knowledge to maximise opportunities in the pipeline * Meeting and exceeding sales targets defined by management **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and Hungarian * Excellent communication skills * Clear articulation over the phone * Experience selling FX forward products in a non\-bank environment * Demonstrable commercial experience, particularly in telesales * A keen interest in developing a career within a high\-performing sales culture Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: FinOps Engineer - 12 Month FTC
description: **Sustainability that means business** **Who We Are** Sustainability software specialist, AMCS, is headquartered in Ireland, with offices in Europe, the USA, and Australasia. With over 1,300 highly\-skilled employees across 22 countries, we specialize in delivering technology solutions to facilitate a carbon neutral future. **What We Do** Our innovative SaaS solutions increase efficiency and boost sustainability in resource\-intensive industries. Over 5,000 customers across 23 countries already benefit from our Performance Sustainability software, ensuring we deliver practical solutions for improved profitability and environmental resilience across the globe. **Our People** AMCS offers team members more than just a job, but an opportunity to map out a career with a company that is growing, evolving and setting out new ways of working that are having a positive impact on the world around us. AMCS was established in Ireland and holds onto those local roots and ‘start\-up’ mentality with a culture of connection. Connection to our work, our customers, our colleagues and our community that creates a working environment that fosters openness, collaboration and creativity. **Role Purpose** The FinOps Engineer will drive financial accountability, cost optimization and operational efficiency across cloud platforms within the Cloud Operations organization. This role ensures visibility into cloud spend, implements cost\-effective provisioning strategies, and balances financial efficiency with engineering agility. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and execute a comprehensive FinOps roadmap aligned with organizational goals. * Establish governance policies for resource utilization, provisioning, and budget approvals. * Financial Accountability * Collaborate with Finance, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure cost transparency and budgeting discipline. * Manage vendor relationships (cloud providers, software suppliers), raise purchase orders, and ensure timely invoice processing. * Operational Excellence * Design and implement a FinOps observability platform to monitor cloud costs and usage. * Integrate cloud cost management tools into the SDLC. * Define and track KPIs for cloud cost optimization and efficiency. * Implement policies for instance rightsizing, resource provisioning, and decommissioning unused resources. * Drive automation initiatives for cost efficiency across cloud environments. * Education \& Awareness * Promote FinOps best practices across engineering teams. * Conduct training sessions to improve cost awareness and financial literacy within technical teams. **Required Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience in FinOps, Cloud Operations, or Cloud Financial Management. * Strong understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and associated cost models. * Experience with cost management tools. * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. * Ability to design governance frameworks and implement automation for cost optimization. **Preferred Qualifications** * FinOps Certified Practitioner or similar certification. * Background in DevOps, SRE, or Cloud Architecture. * Familiarity with budgeting and financial processes in large organizations

title: Finance Counsel
description: **About Wayflyer** Today's small businesses need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer. Our technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. To date, we've deployed over $6bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan. You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world. We have offices in Dublin, London, New York, Charlotte, Berlin and Sydney. **The challenge** Wayflyer moves fast and the legal infrastructure that supports how we fund ourselves and serve our merchants has to move with it. We need a finance lawyer who can hold the complexity of our debt facilities and lending products in one hand, and the needs of our commercial teams in the other. This isn't a role for someone who wants to sit behind a wall of disclaimers. It's for someone who wants to be in the room where decisions get made. **What You'll Actually Do** * You'll be the primary legal voice on everything touching our external funding, implementing and maintaining debt facilities, working through intercreditor arrangements, and making sure our capital markets activity is properly supported end to end. * You'll work alongside our business teams on our core lending and merchant cash advance products, fielding queries from across our geographies and making sure the answers are actually useful to the people asking them. * You'll own our contractual templates and playbook, keeping them current, making them sharper, and rebuilding them when the business has grown past what they were designed for. * When we're launching new products or enhancing existing ones, you'll be the person stress\-testing the legal side before we go live. And you'll manage local counsel across jurisdictions, which means knowing when to delegate, when to push back, and when to roll up your sleeves. **What This Role Could Turn Into** * Finance lawyers at Wayflyer don't stay narrowly defined for long. * This role has a natural path toward leading the legal function for a specific product vertical, or stepping up into a broader General Counsel track as we scale. * There's also a real opportunity to shape how we use AI and legal tech across the team, if you're genuinely curious about that space, you'll have room to lead on it. **Who Thrives Here** * You're the kind of lawyer who gives advice people can actually act on. * You translate complexity without losing the nuance that matters, and you know the difference between managing risk and just saying no. * You don't wait to be asked, if you spot a gap in a template or a problem in a process, you fix it. * You're comfortable with ambiguity and pace, and you've learned that the best legal work often happens outside the strict boundaries of the brief you were given. **We hire for range** * You're a qualified solicitor with around 3–4 years' PQE in banking and finance, whether from a leading firm or a comparable in\-house environment. * You have solid experience across facility documentation, security structuring, and cross\-border financing. * What matters more than where you've been, though, is how you work: commercially minded, transactionally confident, and genuinely interested in what happens when legal meets product. **Location and working policy** **📍** This role is based in our Dublin office, with a hybrid working policy. **The good stuff** 25 days off, plus public holidays. Private healthcare, life insurance, and a pension. Equity \- because you should own a piece of what you're building. Generous parental leave for primary and secondary caregivers. 60 days a year to work abroad from wherever you want. *By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can contact our privacy team at* *privacy@wayflyer.com* *or review our privacy notice at* *https://wayflyer.com/privacy\-notice.*

title: Financial Accountant
description: Based in Dublin West, my client is a well\-established and growing electrical contracting business. In addition to their core contracting operations, their group includes a number of smaller investment companies. They operate with a close\-knit, collaborative office team of circa 10 personnel, which includes a dedicated 3\-person accounts and purchasing function. They are currently seeking a highly experienced, solution\-driven Financial Accountant to manage their day\-to\-day accounts department and ensure all financial functions run seamlessly. **Key Responsibilities** * Produce accurate monthly management accounts for the group. * Maintain the accounts software to ensure it is fully up\-to\-date and reconciled at all times. * Complete all bank and credit card reconciliations. * Prepare year\-end figures and compile a clean, comprehensive accounts file for seamless handover to external auditors. * Prepare, reconcile, and file VAT, PAYE (including BIK), and RCT returns via ROS. * Manage and execute RCT payments via ROS. * Complete and submit required Central Statistics Office (CSO) filings. * Oversee the end\-to\-end Wages and Payroll processing, including managing new starters, leavers, and year\-end payroll requirements. * Manage the day\-to\-day activities of the accounts department, ensuring the team and processes work in perfect sync. * Act as the primary point of contact for external accountants, auditors, customers, and suppliers. * Manage Accounts Payable and creditor control, ensuring accurate supplier payments and statement reconciliations. * Oversee essential company administration, including the annual insurance renewal process and the management of the company fleet vehicles. **Experience \& Qualifications:** * Minimum 5 years of experience in a similar, comprehensive accounting role (ideally within construction, contracting, or a multi\-company group environment). * Fully Qualified Accountant (ACCA, ACA, CIMA) with at least 4 years PQE. * Sage 50 Accounts: Full, expert working knowledge is essential. * Sage Micropay: Full, expert working knowledge is essential. * Deep practical experience with ROS, RCT, and Irish tax compliance is a mandatory requirement. * A professional who works excellently on their own initiative and takes ownership of the finance function. * Strong problem\-solving abilities with a proactive approach to improving processes. * Excellent interpersonal skills for managing a small team and liaising with external stakeholders. **Salary** * Salary €55,000\-€65,000 * 20 days holidays For more information on this role or any other Accountancy roles contact Conal O'Connor or click on the apply button below To view all live jobs with Brightwater and market insights, please visit our website; www.brightwater.ie

title: Financial Accountant
description: **Newly Qualified Accountant – Financial Analyst** This top\-tier global law firm in Dublin 2 is currently recruiting a **Newly Qualified Accountant (ACA / ACCA)** for a Financial Analyst position. As a key member of the Finance team, reporting to the Senior Finance Manager, you will play an important role in enhancing financial control, reporting accuracy, and business insight. This role is ideally suited to a **recently qualified accountant (0–2 years PQE)** with an interest in finance, analytics, and the intersection of finance and IT systems. This is a highly collaborative and cross\-functional role, offering exposure across the wider business and direct engagement with senior stakeholders. **Key Responsibilities** * Drive and maintain monthly management reporting, including analysis against operational KPIs at both departmental and individual fee earner level * Deliver detailed financial analysis to support business decision\-making and performance improvement * Collaborate with the Finance Management Team on presentations, reporting packs, and board\-level reporting * Liaise with Partners and internal stakeholders to respond to finance\-related queries * Support and mentor trainees and junior team members where required * Lead or contribute to finance transformation projects and continuous improvement initiatives * Assist with ad hoc analysis, reporting, and business insights as required * Present KPI performance at individual fee earner level * Act as a key liaison with the internal IT team in relation to the practice management system * Support system enhancements, implementations, and process improvements **About You** * Recently qualified ACA / ACCA (0–2 years post\-qualification experience) * Strong academic background and technical accounting knowledge * Excellent analytical, reconciliation, and problem\-solving skills * Strong attention to detail with advanced IT and Excel capabilities * Exposure to systems, reporting tools, or implementations is an advantage * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Able to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment * Proactive, organised, and able to manage multiple priorities effectively **Why Join Us** * Excellent salary and benefits package * Hybrid working model * Strong exposure to senior stakeholders in a leading international law firm * Excellent scope for career development and internal progression **How to Apply** To apply, please contact Richard Minchin at Lex Consultancy.

title: Forward Deployed Engineer
description: **At Poka, we’re transforming how frontline teams operate, learn, and solve problems.** **Our connected worker platform is purpose\-built for manufacturing, empowering frontline workers to share knowledge, collaborate in real\-time, and drive continuous improvement. Think of it as the missing digital link, bringing the human interface into the stack of the Smart Factory, generating the right data and insights that drive entirely new scales of process improvements.** **We’re proud to support global leaders like Nestlé, Bosch, Mars, and Danone in their digital transformation journeys and as part of the IFS family, our reach and impact continue to grow globally.** **You don’t just scope solutions \- you build what others can’t.** As a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) at Poka, you’ll work directly with leading manufacturers to identify, design, and deliver high\-impact AI and integration solutions inside real operations. This isn’t a traditional solutions or consulting role. You’ll own outcomes end\-to\-end. From discovery through to working software, helping customers move from idea to deployed solution in weeks. Working with Poka’s AI team, who have already designed and built agents, workflows, and prototypes that solve real problems using customer data, systems, and processes. Your role will be bringing AI to life in operational environments and driving acceleration with our global, enterprise customers. **What you’ll be doing** * Partner with customers to deeply understand their operations, systems, and priorities — and identify where AI and integrations can unlock value * Lead discovery sessions and technical discussions, shaping high\-impact use cases and defining realistic solution approaches * Design and build integrations between Poka and enterprise systems (ERP, MES, CMMS, etc.) using APIs and middleware tools * Develop AI\-powered workflows and agents (e.g., operator assistants, intelligent triage, document processing, anomaly detection) using modern LLM frameworks * Own delivery end\-to\-end — from scoping and architecture to implementation, testing, and handoff * Support strategic deals by contributing to pre\-sales conversations, workshops, and technical deep dives * Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and Services to refine solutions and translate field learnings into scalable capabilities * Contribute reusable integration templates and AI workflow patterns that can scale across customers * Travel to customer sites (:25–35%) for workshops, discovery, and key delivery moments **What you bring** * Experience in a customer\-facing technical role (e.g., Solutions Architect, Sales Engineer, Integration Consultant, or similar) * Strong hands\-on experience building AI agents, LLM\-powered workflows, or automation solutions — this is your core strength * Practical experience working with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic) and building real\-world use cases (not just experimentation) * Solid grounding in system integrations, APIs, and connecting enterprise platforms * Ability to quickly go from ambiguous problem → working prototype using tools like Python, Node.js, and modern AI stacks * Confidence bridging business and technical conversations, from frontline operations to IT stakeholders * Strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to adapt solutions to real\-world constraints * Comfort working in ambiguity and helping shape a new, evolving capability within Poka * Familiarity with manufacturing systems or industrial environments is a strong plus **Why Poka** * **Build, don’t just advise** – You own delivery and ship real solutions, not slide decks * **AI with real\-world impact** – Apply cutting\-edge AI in live manufacturing environments, not theoretical use cases * **Speed matters** – Your work goes from idea to deployed solution in weeks * **Shape what comes next** – The solutions you build directly influence Poka’s product roadmap and future AI capabilities * **High\-impact customers** – Work with global manufacturers solving complex, meaningful operational challenges If you’re someone who thrives on building, experimenting, and turning emerging AI capabilities into real\-world impact — this is where that work actually happens. **What we offer:** * Dynamic and collaborative work environment. * Generous vacation policy upon arrival. * Remote work policy. * A truly flexible schedule that allows you to adapt your work hours. * Benefits Plan offered from day one. * MacBook Pro and €500 allowance for your home office. * Generous referral bonus. * The tools, support, and opportunities you need to keep learning and progressing in your career. *Want to learn more about our culture? Click HERE.* **Please note:** An understanding of English is essential at Poka, as it is the language used during company\-wide meetings and many internal communications, ensuring effective collaboration and decision\-making. In addition, as Poka operates in an international environment, English is the primary language for communicating with our clients and partners, enabling us to better serve them and represent Poka in a professional manner.

title: General Dentist
description: Principal Associate Dentist **Full\-time \| Flexible Sessions Available** Earn €125,000–€230,000\+ per year while focusing on dentistry, not administration. We are an established, 100% Irish\-owned dental group with more than 40 years of experience and a growing network of clinics across Dublin and the East of Ireland. This is an opportunity to join a modern, patient\-focused group where the operational burden is handled for you, allowing you to concentrate on delivering excellent clinical care and developing your career. What You'll Receive * On\-target earnings from **€125,000 in Year 1**, with potential to exceed **€230,000 per year** as your patient list develops * Direct mentorship and support from our Clinical Director * Senior clinical leadership opportunities * Flexible sessional working * Funded CPD and group development programmes * State\-of\-the\-art clinics featuring modern equipment including digital scanning and advanced technologies * Dedicated operational and administrative support teams * Marketing support to help maintain a busy diary and strong patient flow * Professionally trained reception and treatment coordinator teams to support patient conversion and care delivery What We Handle * Accounts and administration * Bookkeeping and payroll * Compliance and paperwork * Group purchasing and supplier negotiations * Marketing and patient acquisition * Operational management and clinic support You focus on clinical excellence.....we take care of the rest. title: General Operative - Nightshift
description: * Pack out \& lining orders, bulk retrieval, order processing, fridge \& general assembly, order scanning, putting away stock, completion of van sheets –In line with Uniphar Standard operating procedures. * Unload pallets from Dublin truck using forklift, confirm quantities \& line in run sequence. * Work on the IWACS system * Work with the drivers EPOD system and have working knowledge of same. * Unload pallets for goods inwards when required. * Liaise with Citywest warehouse if required. * Process empty totes / lids for return to Citywest. * Record any issues in the hand over book for shift supervisor/manager * Work with the Supervisor and Line Leader to process Bulk Stock and/or assemble customer orders in an efficient and accurate manner **Key Skills \& Experiences** * Can do attitude. * Very good verbal and written communication skills. * Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team. * High level of attention to detail. * Strong Customer Focus. * Respect, collaborative approach. * Highlight operational improvements to enhance service and productivity. * Basic Computer skills **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: General Operative / B Driver - Traffic Management (Blanchardstown)
description: Interested in a career in traffic management? If you believe you have the skills and experience required for this role, please review the details below and click the **Apply button** to apply. **Traffic Management Operative \- B driver** GKTM is a traffic management company providing traffic management solutions nationwide, having operated at the forefront of the industry over the past decade, working on many large infrastructure and transport projects. **Role** We currently have a number of vacancies for Traffic Management Operatives B driver to be based primarily in our Blanchardstown Depot. As a **Traffic Management Operative \- Driver**, you will play a vital role in supporting the safe and efficient movement of traffic around roadworks, maintenance projects, and special events. Your primary responsibility will be driving company vehicles to transport traffic management equipment, materials, and personnel to and from work sites. You will also assist with the safe loading, unloading, installation, maintenance, and removal of traffic control measures, including signs, cones, barriers, and temporary traffic management systems. You will ensure all work is carried out in accordance with company procedures, traffic management plans, and health and safety regulations. The role requires a strong commitment to safety, excellent driving skills, reliability, and the ability to work effectively as part of a team. You will also interact professionally with clients, contractors, and members of the public while representing the company on site and on the road. **Training** will be delivered upon commencement to ensure the successful candidate understands how to carry out their duties safely while on site. The successful candidate will look forward to the following: **Benefits** * Competitive rates of pay * Opportunities to progress within company * Company organised events * In depth traffic management training, both in\-house and externally * Full\-time, permanent positions are available * Overtime available **Requirements** * Valid Safe Pass * Manual Handling * Full B driver license * Own transport essential for shifts which may begin outside of the hours of public transport **Responsibilities:** * **Traffic Management Equipment Setup:** Safely install and set up traffic management equipment, signs, cones, barriers, and other equipment according to established plans and procedures under the guidance of the site supervisor. * **Traffic Management:** Monitor traffic flow and respond to changing conditions, adjusting traffic control measures as necessary to ensure the safety of both workers and the public. * **Driving Company Vehicles:** Operate company vehicles, such as trucks or vans, to transport traffic management equipment and personnel to and from work sites. Drive vehicles with caution, obeying all traffic laws and company policies, and ensure the safe loading and securing of equipment. * **Safety Compliance:** Strictly adhere to all safety regulations, policies, and guidelines while carrying out duties, including wearing appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE). * **Equipment Maintenance:** Perform routine checks and maintenance of traffic management equipment to ensure proper functionality, reporting any issues to the supervisor. * **Communication:** Effectively communicate with colleagues, supervisors, and the public, providing clear and courteous instructions and information regarding traffic control measures and diversions. * **Emergency Response:** Act promptly and effectively in case of emergencies or incidents, following established procedures to mitigate risks and ensure public safety. * **Documentation:** Maintain accurate records and documentation related to traffic management activities, including equipment usage and maintenance reports. * **Environmental Awareness:** Consider and implement environmentally friendly practices in traffic management, including waste management and pollution control. * **Continuous Improvement:** Stay updated on industry best practices and participate in training programs to improve skills and knowledge related to traffic management. **Profile of the ideal candidate:** * Previous experience in traffic management or a related field is preferred but not essential. * Flexible, willing to work shifts during the day or night in line with the requirements of our clients. * Punctual. * Willing to work down the country for up to five consecutive days. * React positively to feedback provided by your mentor and other leaders within the organisation. * Strong attention to detail and the ability to work efficiently and safely in a fast\-paced environment. * Excellent communication and teamwork skills. * Physical fitness and the ability to lift heavy equipment and work outdoors in various weather conditions. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €14\.50 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking Experience: * General Operative: 1 year (required) Licence/Certification: * own transport for early commute (required) * Manual Handling (required) * Safe Pass (required) * Full B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

machinery, plant machinery, or heavy equipment manufacturing (desirable) * Good knowledge of hydraulic systems and components * Experience with hydraulic plumbing and wiring * Ability to read and understand hydraulic and electrical schematics * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills * Reliable, punctual, and well organised **Progression \& Development:** Robinson is committed to
