title: IT Solutions Architect
description: **Job Title** IT Solutions Architect **Salary** €76, 202 pa (Other arrangements may apply of you are currently a serving civil or public servant) **Tenure** 5\-year fixed term contract **Work Location** Dublin office **Closing Date** Midnight, Thursday 2nd July 2026 **Role Profile** We have an exciting opportunity in SEAI for a Solutions Architect in the IT Department which is part of the Corporate Services Directorate. The IT Department provides support across all aspects of SEAI operations, with teams focused on several areas: IT Project and Business System Programmes (PMO), Enterprise Architecture, Data Governance, IT Operations including IT Helpdesk, IT Governance, IT Change Management, and Cyber and Information Security. The successful candidate will join the current Enterprise Architect and support them in defining, maintaining, and governing the SEAIs IT systems architecture to ensure that business strategy objectives, and costeffective technology are aligned. Reporting to the Chief Information Officer (CIO), the successful candidate will work across the IT Department and third\-party vendors to develop and integrate ICT systems, reviewing existing systems to identify areas for improvement and monitoring the outcome of new and upgrades business systems. The successful candidate will join an enthusiastic and innovative team who are committed to the high\-quality delivery of SEAI's ambitious ICT Strategy and customer centric digital services. **Essential Requirements** The successful candidate must be able to demonstrate: * A degree (minimum Level 8 on the NFQ or higher) in IT, Computer Science, or other relevant discipline. * A minimum of 5 years directly relevant experience working in IT Architecture design and implementation within large complex organisations (a master's degree may account for 1\-years' experience). * Demonstrated proficiency in system architecture principles and frameworks, software development practices, cloud platforms (Azure) and service orientated architecture (SOA), data architecture and software development practices. * Experience in establishing and operating architecture frameworks and governance. * Significant experience in stakeholder management involving a diverse range of internal and external stakeholders with complex interdependencies. * Proficiency in development methodologies such as DevOps. **Desirable Requirements** * Certificate in architecture frameworks such as TOGAF. * Experience in enterprise\-level systems design. * Experience in microservices and API first architectures. * Proficiency in the Irish Language. * Experience or knowledge of delivering positive customer experiences. **Eligibility to Work** The SEAI has a legal obligation to ensure that all employees are lawfully entitled to work in Ireland. To qualify, candidates must hold a valid visa on the date of application and where applicable to support a candidate's application, candidates must submit a valid work permit/visa confirming permission to work in Ireland when requested. Failure to submit the required evidence, and failure to hold a valid permit/visa during the period of the contract, will result in the application and/or contract of employment being rendered void. Note in respect of UK citizens: Information regarding the Common Travel Area is available **here** .

title: Immigration Legal Executive
description: A well\-established and growing Dublin law firm is seeking an experienced Immigration Legal Executive to join its busy practice. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated candidate to work as part of a specialist team handling a broad range of immigration matters. **Key Responsibilities** * Managing immigration files from initial instruction through to conclusion. * Preparing and submitting applications relating to visas, residence permissions, citizenship, family reunification, employment permits, and international protection matters. * Liaising with clients, government departments, and relevant agencies. * Drafting correspondence, submissions, and legal documentation. * Maintaining accurate file records and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. * Assisting solicitors with immigration\-related litigation and appeals where required. **Key Requirements** * Minimum of two years' experience working across all aspects of Irish immigration law. * Strong knowledge of current immigration legislation, policies, and procedures. * Excellent organisational and case management skills. * Strong written and verbal communication abilities. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and legal case management systems. * Not suitable for FE1 candidates. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: In-Game Specialist (DNA)
description: **Who We Are** At 2K, we create some of the most iconic and culture\-shaping video games in entertainment, including ***NBA® 2K***, one of the top\-selling franchises in the world, and legendary titles like ***BioShock®****,* ***Borderlands®****,* ***Mafia****,* ***Sid Meier's Civilization*****®**, and***XCOM®***, as well as fan favorites ***WWE® 2K****,* ***TopSpin®***, and ***PGA TOUR® 2K****.* We build unforgettable experiences by pushing the boundaries of creativity, authenticity and innovation across every genre. Our portfolio is brought to life by some of the most influential game development studios in the world. **Visual Concepts, Firaxis Games, Hangar 13, Cat Daddy Games, 31st Union, Cloud Chamber, Gearbox, HB Studios,** and **2K SportsLab** create world\-class experiences across platforms. But what truly powers 2K is our people. We believe the best ideas come from teams that feel empowered, supported, and inspired. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive workplace where people are encouraged to **come as they are** and do their best work. **What We Need** The In\-Game Specialist is the operational and strategic lead for the in\-game messaging lifecycle within DNA titles. The Specialist operationalizes the in\-game weekly ad schedule and storefront offers. This role is responsible for the end\-to\-end orchestration of targeted marketing and lifecycle campaigns across messaging and commerce, including but not limited to upsell and player engagement journeys, partnership activations, and locker code delivery. Acting as a central hub between Marketing, Product, Insights, Joint Tech (T2/2KT) and Game Studios. In this remit, the specialist also plays an important role in the coordination of pre\- and post\-launch in\-game QA testing to ensure successful delivery of in\-game messaging and storefronts. A critical component of this P4 role involves scaling in\-game DNA tech capabilities in partnership with 2KT to unlock greater commercial levers as well as driving the continuous refinement of team processes to ensure successful delivery at scale. This individual provides functional mentorship to rotating graduates while driving operational excellence across the Owned Media team. **What You'll Do** The In\-Game Specialist is the operational and strategic lead for the in\-game messaging lifecycle within DNA titles. The Specialist operationalizes the in\-game weekly ad schedule and storefront offers. This role is responsible for the end\-to\-end orchestration of targeted marketing and lifecycle campaigns across messaging and commerce, including but not limited to upsell and player engagement journeys, partnership activations, and locker code delivery. Acting as a central hub between Marketing, Product, Insights, Joint Tech (T2/2KT) and Game Studios. In addition, the specialist plays an important role in the coordination of pre\- and post\-launch in\-game QA testing and the scaling of in\-game DNA tech capabilities. This individual provides functional mentorship to rotating graduates while driving operational excellence across the Owned Media team. **What Will Make You A Great Fit** Ad/Storefront Operations \& Strategy * Strategy \& Scheduling: Execute the weekly in\-game messaging calendar and set up in\-game storefront offers, as well as supporting a wide range of targeted upsell and engagement activations. * End\-to\-End Operationalization: Manage the technical setup, scheduling, and deployment of all in\-game messaging and storefronts, ensuring 100% accuracy in a high\-stakes, live\-service environment. * Performance Optimization: Partner with the Insights team to understand impact of in\-game interventions and refine in\-game strategy. Process Refinement \& Scalability * Workflow Optimization: Evaluate and refine existing team processes to eliminate bottlenecks and ensure the successful delivery of activations as the portfolio and complexity scale. * Standardization: Develop and maintain standardized templates, documentation, and playbooks for in\-game ad and storefront operations. * Scalability Initiatives: Identify and implement tools or automated solutions that increase the team's capacity to manage high\-volume activations. DNA Tech Scalability * Strategy \& Workflow Optimization: Identifying strategic opportunities to unlock commercial \& engagement levers and streamline internal workstreams through DNA tech. * Tech Briefing: Submitting detailed tech briefs for new feature development \& improvements to existing features and submitting bugs on an ongoing basis to develop DNA tech capabilities. * Scalability \& Standardization: Ensure long term scalability of all tech requests and standardization across all DNA integrated titles. Product/Marketing \& Developer Collaboration * Product/Marketing Integration: Collaborate closely with the BU Product \& Marketing teams to synchronize in\-game activations with external campaigns, partnerships, and cross\-sell initiatives. * Studio Developer Liaison: Act as the primary operational point of contact for studios such as HB Studios, Hangar 13, VC (WWE), and Firaxis. * Technical Troubleshooting: Work directly with developer studios on bug identification and the continuous optimization of in\-game tech to improve the player experience. Functional Leadership * Mentorship: Oversee the day\-to\-day work of rotating graduates, providing task\-level guidance and ensuring operational excellence. * Functional Expert: Serve as the subject matter expert for in\-game operations, advising internal stakeholders on feasibility and best practices. Core Competencies * Behavioral: Strong sense of urgency and ownership; ability to maintain high precision under tight weekly deadlines. * Functional / Technical: Expert knowledge of in\-game messaging \& commerce technology (Technodrome), targeted campaign frameworks, and tech briefing. * Operational Excellence \& Scalability: Proven ability to build scalable workflows and improve organizational efficiency. * Strategic Influence: Ability to align cross\-functional stakeholders (Marketing, Product, Dev, Insights) toward a unified in\-game strategy. * Collaboration: Proven ability to build strong working relationships with marketing, product, and technical teams alike. Required Qualifications, Knowledge, and Job\-Related Skills * Extensive experience in digital operations, live\-service gaming, or in\-game messaging management. * Proven experience in process improvement or operational scaling within a marketing or technical environment. * Track record of managing a high\-volume, weekly operational cadence. * Experience in working with technical teams to deliver scalable UI \& functionality product improvements * Understanding of video game launch \& release management processes and experience working with Functional QA teams to validate player\-facing product features * Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data insights and pivot strategy accordingly * Excellent project management skills, specifically in coordinating between Marketing and Technical/Development teams. * Self\-motivated with the ability to operate independently at a P4 seniority level. Preferred Qualifications * Passion for our player's experience in\-game and an understanding of video games, as well as key marketing principles. * Experience working within a global publishing organization. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to ensuring that qualified individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform their essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us if you need reasonable accommodation. Please note that 2K Games and its studios never uses instant messaging apps or personal email accounts to contact prospective employees or conduct interviews and when emailing, only use 2K.com accounts. \#LI\-Hybrid

title: InfoSec Analyst I
description: MongoDB’s Security Assurance team is on a mission to make MongoDB one of the world’s most trusted technology vendors. We are doing this in two ways. First, we provide customers with the clear, concise and accurate information about the security of our products that customers require to use our products. Second, we serve as trusted security SMEs to MongoDB’s field teams. The InfoSec Analyst I is an independent contributor role. This role is vital to the Security Assurance team’s mission and is responsible for coordinating completion of customer due diligence reviews as well as helping MongoDB’s field personnel answer complex customer security questions. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model. Responsibilities: * Actively manage the Assurance team’s queue to ensure requests are handled promptly * Complete customer security reviews. Help other team members complete reviews as necessary * Help customer facing personnel respond to customer security questions * Monitor relevant channels and answer in scope questions when possible, or escalate to more senior team members * Proactively maintain the team’s knowledgebase * Proactively recommend improvements in team processes and tooling to team leadership * Share relevant and actionable customer feedback with team leadership * Help coordinate development of customer facing response to high\-profile supply chain events including critical severity CVEs that may affect MongoDB or its vendors * Lead complex projects to help build, scale and train the team * Serve as an internal subject matter expert on MongoDB product security topics, assurance trends, customer concerns, and emerging regulations * Design and deliver internal enablement training on security posture and compliance topics for teams across the organization Requirements: * 2 to 3 years of experience in Information Security * BS in Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience * Direct exposure to at least one major framework or regulation such as ISO 27001, SOC2, CSA or DORA is preferred * Basic understanding of public cloud technologies including IAM, encryption, network security and tenant isolation * Strong communication skills, with the ability to present security concepts to technical and non\-technical stakeholders up to and including at the executive levels * Ability to coordinate and manage multiple urgent requests simultaneously while effectively collaborating with SMEs across various time zones * A self\-starter with a strong desire to grow into a trusted authority on the security of MongoDB and its products Success in this Role Means: * Within 3 months \- Have a solid understanding of MongoDB’s product portfolio, core security posture, and the Security Assurance team’s procedures and tooling; reliably triage incoming requests; independently complete customer security reviews with guidance from more senior team members; monitor key internal channels and answer straightforward in‑scope questions * Within 6 months \- Independently own end‑to‑end completion of most standard customer security reviews; serve as a trusted partner to customer‑facing teams by helping them respond to complex security questions; make measurable contributions to the team knowledgebase and playbooks; proactively identify and help implement improvements to team processes and tooling * Within 12 months \- Be recognized as a trusted subject matter expert on MongoDB’s product portfolio, security posture and common customer concerns; complete complex projects that materially improve review quality, throughput, or customer experience; consistently surface relevant, actionable customer feedback that can influence Security roadmaps **About MongoDB** MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the data platform for the AI era, enabling builders to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified data platform, the most widely available, globally distributed data platform on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud\-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi\-cloud data platform and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. With offices worldwide and over 67,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI\-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software. title: Information Technology Specialist
description: **SENIOR ICT SPECIALIST (HIGHER EXECUTIVE OFFICER)** The State Laboratory is seeking a permanent Senior ICT Specialist to support and enhance its ICT infrastructure, applications and cybersecurity environment. Reporting to the ICT Section Manager, the successful candidate will play a key role in delivering ICT projects, driving service improvements and ensuring the reliability and security of business\-critical systems. **Key responsibilities include:** * Support and maintain ICT services comprising network, storage, compute, virtualisation, endpoint and cloud services. * Perform infrastructure incident management, escalation and response. * Champion security best practices, taking a lead role in security applications including endpoint protection, mail filters, web filters and firewalls. * Assist the ICT Manager in the development and execution of the Cybersecurity Strategy * Coordinate, support and review assigned work of ICT support staff where required. **Essential Requirements:** Given the technical nature of the role to be filled, you must have a relevant qualification which is matched with experience appropriate to this Senior ICT Specialist role. It is up to you to make sure that your CV showcases the experience you have gained so far. Accordingly, you must, on or before Thursday, 16th July 2026 meet one of the following sets of criteria: * Hold a degree qualification at Level 7 on the National Framework of Qualifications (NFQ) * major award (i.e. an ordinary level degree), or higher, in a relevant computing or * computational discipline and at least 3 years directly relevant\* hands\-on ICT networks and * cyber security experience from your employment to date. * OR * Hold a degree qualification at Level 8 on the NFQ major award (i.e. honours degree), or * higher, with computing or computational modules taken in the final year and at least 3 * years directly relevant\* hands\-on ICT networks and cyber security experience from your * employment to date. * OR * Hold a major award qualification of at least Level 6, or higher, on the National Framework of * Qualifications (NFQ) in a relevant computing or computational discipline, for example: an * NFQ Level 6 qualification from a Post Leaving Cert Course at an ETB, or the FIT Tech * Apprenticeship and at least 5 years directly relevant\* hands\-on ICT networks and cyber * security experience from your employment to date. * OR * Have achieved at least two industry\-recognised certifications one of which must be in an * area relevant to this role (see page 5 for a range of examples) and at least 5 years directly * relevant\* hands\-on ICT networks and cyber security experience from your employment to * date. * * \*Examples of relevant experience should be appropriate to the role and include, but are not limited to, areas such as networking or cyber security solution implementation, configuration and support/ administration including WAN/ LAN, enterprise firewalls and perimeter defence infrastructure, and penetration testing. **How to Apply** 1\. Download the Information Booklet below: **Job Information Booklet (Download)** 2\. Candidates must apply with their CV via the link below. **Apply Here** \*We require a CV and the completed online application form to be submitted in order for your application to be accepted **Closing Date** 16/07/2026 – 17:00 Applications will not be accepted after the closing date

title: Insurance Apprenticeship - Drogheda
description: Based in our Drogheda office, Howden Ireland are delighted to partner with the Atlantic Technological University Sligo and the Insurance Institute of Ireland for the Insurance apprenticeship programme. If you would like to join the apprenticeship programme, we would love to hear from you. Find out more below: About the Apprenticeship Opportunity We are looking for ambitious and talented individuals who show the potential to develop into a high performing business professional. This is an apprentice scheme that will allow the successful candidate exposure to all aspects of our business, allowing you to develop your technical expertise as well as professional skills. While on the programme, in addition to gaining on the job insurance experience, you will complete the insurance industry professional qualifications APA and CIP as well as a Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in Insurance Practice (Apprenticeship) which is Level 8 on the NFQ awarded by IT Sligo. The apprenticeship programme is a work\-based learning programme and requires timely submission of on\-the\-job assessments, attendance at and successful attainment of IT Sligo and CIP exams as part of the programme. When you join Howden, you will be assigned a dedicated business supervisor and mentor to provide support throughout the scheme while always been given on the job continuous training. We will work with you to become a valuable contributor in the success of our business, developing your own rewarding career in the insurance industry while completing the three\-year Level 8 Bachelor's Degree course. In return, we expect you to bring the commitment and drive to add significant value to the company. About the Role: As an apprentice you will gain in\-depth knowledge of risks and the insurance market in order to arrange suitable policies \& cover for your clients. You will learn how to be an effective intermediary between our clients and insurance companies. Some Responsibilities are noted below: * Gather information from your clients, assessing their insurance needs and risk profile * Research insurance companies' policies and negotiate with underwriters to find the most suitable insurance for your clients at the best price * Ensure that your clients understand the terms and the extent of the cover provided in line with industry regulations * Foresee your clients' insurance needs, such as policy renewals or amendments * Advise your clients on whether and when they need to make a claim on their policies * Arrange specialised types of insurance cover in complex cases; this may involve preparing reports for insurance underwriters and surveyors and negotiating with insurers * Advise your clients on risk management and help to devise new ways to mitigate risks, for example, by adding security measures such as fencing, surveillance cameras or lighting to commercial properties to reduce the likelihood of a break\-in * Build and maintain ongoing relationships with your clients The Apprenticeship programme is a work\-based learning programme and requires timely submission of on the job assessments, attendance at and successful attainment of ATU Sligo and CIP exams as part of the programme. About You When you work in insurance your customers place a lot of trust and responsibility in your hands, so its essential that you have the necessary knowledge and skills to give them peace of mind. Some of the key attributes that the individual should have are: * Ability to communicate effectively (in both written and verbal form) * Eager to learn and want to develop within an exciting industry * Proactive approach to building and managing customer relationships * Ability to prioritise and work under time pressures * Focused, Diligent and committed to the apprenticeship and company * Excellent PC skills \- Microsoft Packages * Willing to be part of a team while working independently If you want to be part of a dynamic work environment while being rewarded for your creativity, innovation and determination, start your career with Howden. Key Questions asked before applying Interested individuals should ask themselves the following questions before applying: * Do I meet the minimum entry level requirements? Entry\-Requirements\-Updated.pdf (iii.ie) * Can I commit to a three\-year contract of apprenticeship? * Do I have the self\-motivation and time management skills to study at a BA (Hons) level and work at the same time? * Can I take ownership and accountability for my own actions and responsibilities? Application Process * Please submit your application via our careers page * Planned start date: beginning of August 2026 or sooner if available * Closing date for applications is the 7th of July About Howden Ireland Howden Ireland is part of Howden, the global insurance intermediary group, and has over 600 employees across 29 local offices advising clients on their commercial insurance, personal insurance, health insurance, life and pensions and mortgage requirements. Our success is primarily down to the incredible people we employ. For more information, please visit About Us \| Howden Ireland (howdengroup.com)

title: Internal Auditor (FinCrime)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Audit team is critical to our business. They work closely with Risk and Compliance to create internal risk management and compliance processes, and make sure they’re running efficiently. We're looking for a motivated Internal Auditor who specialises in financial crime to support regulatory audits. You’ll be executing internal audits and adapting your skills to auditing new subject matter. Up to shape what’s next in finance? Let’s get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Developing and executing audit programmes under internal policies, regulatory requirements, and professional standards * Optimising data usage in the audit process to enhance risk understanding and deliver pertinent insights * Providing input and assisting in identifying relevant risks and regulatory requirements for audit scopes * Interacting with staff to obtain a comprehensive understanding of relevant risks, controls, and processes * Communicating and discussing findings directly with business unit management * Developing audit reports that identify deficiencies and root causes while providing recommendations that add value * Conducting research and completing assigned training requirements to maintain professional relevance * Partnering with business units to develop recommendations for process optimisation, internal control, and compliance * Performing follow\-up reviews of deficiencies noted during previous audits * Monitoring audit progress against timelines and managing projects to completion, escalating where required **What You'll Need** * 3\+ years of experience in an audit\-related role within a regulated financial services environment * Comprehensive knowledge of the current regulatory environment, including EU money laundering directives, JMLSG, and FATF guidance * Familiarity with financial crime controls, such as KYC/KYB, due diligence, transaction monitoring, and sanctions screening * Motivation to learn, grow, and have an immediate impact as part of an expanding team **Nice to have** * Experience in a fast\-paced environment with a focus on execution * An undergraduate or master's degree in a quantitative discipline * Proficiency working with and interpreting data using SQL, SAS, or Python Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Java SRE
description: Java SRE Location: Dublin, Ireland (3 Days per Week Onsite) Contract Length: 2 Years Rate: Up to €380 per day Our client who is a global leader in the payments technology industry, operating across 210\+ countries and territories. This organization is driving innovation in digital payments, helping businesses, financial institutions, governments, and consumers transact safely, securely, and efficiently worldwide. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced Java SRE / Full Stack Java Engineer to join a high\-performing team delivering enterprise\-scale applications and services in a fast\-paced, technology\-driven environment. **Key Skills \& Experience Required:** * Strong experience with Java JDK 8 * Spring Boot, Spring Security, Maven, Hibernate/JPA * RESTful APIs, JSON \& SOAP\-based services * SQL Server and relational database technologies * Multi\-threading, Message Queuing \& Distributed Systems * Design Patterns and Enterprise Application Development * JUnit and automated testing frameworks * Git and Jenkins * Knowledge of Cloud technologies (AWS, Cloud Foundry, or similar) * React, Redux \& TypeScript What You'll Bring: * Strong troubleshooting and production support capabilities * Experience working in highly scalable, mission\-critical environments * Passion for reliability, automation, performance, and continuous improvement * Ability to collaborate effectively within cross\-functional Agile teams If you're interested in learning more, please apply directly or send your CV for a confidential discussion.

title: Junior Account Executive - Start a New Journey!
description: * Control your Financial Future – Exceptional Rewards * Industry\-Recognised Training Programme * Defined Career Path \& Accelerated Progression Are you a dynamic go\-getter ready to kickstart your career in the fast\-paced world of sales? Look no further – we're on the hunt for a Junior Account Executive to join our passionate and innovative team! **Who We Are:** Access Healthcare is one of Ireland’s fastest\-growing healthcare recruitment agencies, expanding across Northern Ireland and scaling rapidly. We are building a team of driven individuals who want more than a job; they want a career where performance, growth, and earning potential are fully aligned. **As part of this role, you will:** * Conduct in\-depth market research to identify potential clients, market trends, and competitive analysis to gain a competitive edge. * Develop and implement effective lead\-generation strategies to identify and pursue new business opportunities. * Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with existing and prospective clients to understand their needs and tailor solutions to meet their specific requirements. * Lead negotiations with clients to secure profitable deals, working towards mutually beneficial agreements. **Why our team loves Access:** * Uncapped Commission Structure: We offer an uncapped commission structure, giving you the opportunity to earn a generous commission on top of your base salary. * Tailored Benefits Package: We care about the well\-being of our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits package includes medical insurance, pension, gym membership, and more! * Wellness Program: Your health and wellness matter to us. We provide a wellness program designed to encourage a balanced and healthy lifestyle. This may include access to fitness classes, breakfast club, and talks from experts including financial advisors. * Team Events and Outings: Throughout the year, we organize various team events and outings, creating opportunities for you to relax, bond with colleagues, and celebrate our collective achievements. * Supportive and Friendly Team Environment: At Access, you'll be part of a supportive and friendly team. We value open communication, collaboration, and mutual respect. Our inclusive work environment encourages creativity, innovation, and personal growth. **What we look for:** * A bachelor’s degree. * Fluent English. * Metrics\-driven mindset. * 12 months of experience – any background can be considered! * A positive attitude and a proven ability to perform. I specialise in recruiting for our teams, so if you wish to discuss this or other opportunities, please feel free to contact francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie I specialise in recruiting for our teams (Archer and Access), so if you wish to discuss this or other opportunities, please feel free to contact francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare

title: Junior Architectural Technologist - Dublin Studio
description: **Role:** Graduate Architectural Technologist **Client:** Highly Regarded Irish Studio **Location:** Dublin City **Salary:** Competitive Package **Our Client** Our client is a highly regarded architectural practice with offices across Ireland. They have a strong track record delivering projects across the Commercial, Residential, Education and Hospitality sectors, and have been in business for over two decades. With a growing project pipeline and an expanding Dublin studio, they offer an excellent opportunity for an ambitious Architectural Technologist to develop their career within a collaborative and supportive environment. **The Role** Our client is seeking a Junior or Graduate Architectural Technologist to join their growing Dublin team, supporting the delivery of projects across a range of sectors. Working alongside experienced Designers, you will assist in the production of technical drawings and BIM models, contributing to projects from planning through to construction. This role offers excellent exposure to all stages of project delivery while providing ongoing mentoring and support to help develop your technical and professional skills. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to work within a collaborative studio environment, gaining hands\-on experience on a variety of high\-quality projects while developing their Revit and technical detailing capabilities. **Required Background** * BSc (Hons) in Architectural Technology or a related discipline * Graduate or up to 2 years' professional experience within an architectural practice * Proficiency in Revit and a good understanding of BIM principles * Strong technical aptitude and an eagerness to develop construction detailing skills * Exposure to the Irish or UK construction industry advantageous * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Collaborative approach with the ability to work as part of a multidisciplinary team * Well organised, with the ability to manage deadlines and prioritise workload * Enthusiastic, proactive and keen to build a long\-term career in Architectural Technology **The Package** Excellent salary on offer, as well as 23 days annual leave, up to 2 days WFH post\-probation and other perks.

title: Junior Associate - Investment Servicing
description: Summary: The Junior Associate will assist the Middle Office team in the development of large\-scale projects for high profile platforms, from Management Companies to fund clients and work in a supportive role within the Trade Execution and Investment Servicing teams. * Adhere to and follow the middle office checklist daily, including but not limited to Corporate Actions, Data Checks, Subscriptions and Redemption activity, Unfilled Order and Pending Instructions, Failed Trades, New Order Instructions and Reconciliations. * Assist the Manager with the design and implementation of internal and operating controls (especially in relation to OMS and PMS systems and all interactions with the market. * As you grown, learn to evaluate the risk involved in each situation, prioritize risk levels and escalate as appropriate. * Assist the team in ensuring all securities are settled on time and failed trades are investigated. * Currency Hedging including the daily production of the hedge fund workbook for review by the supervisor and instruction of hedge adjustments required which must be approved by supervisor. * Daily reconciliation between Middle Office records and depositary/custodian holdings. * Liaise daily with the trade execution team to ensure the trade execution, settlement and reconciliation process is adhered to. * Follow all pre\-trade compliance procedures and checks, escalate breaches. * Assist in the on\-boarding of new clients, broker and depositary/custodians. * As instructed by your client and approved by the supervisor, structure and process all payments across multiple platforms and service providers. * Ensure all corporate actions (elective and mandatory) are processed and accounted for. * Actively participate in fund governance meetings and attend technical training. * Assists colleagues when capacity permits and notifies supervisor with availability. * Foster positive team environment and ensure open communication. * Actively participate in team projects as instructed by the supervisor. * Develop and maintain detailed knowledge of Waystone operations and the regulatory implications thereof. * Adhere to Waystone change management procedures in order to ensure efficient implementations and minimal client impact. * Assist the team in the development, implementation and monitoring of policies and procedural manuals that contribute to the efficiency of the department while also ensuring timely and accurate delivery of service and regulatory compliance. * Ensure all regulatory and compliance requirements are met for an assigned portfolio of clients. * Communicate with clients to ensure satisfaction with the service provided and to resolve any potential issues. * When instructed, independently liaise with fund service providers and fund legal counsels. To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. **Education**: * A third level degree in Business, finance and/or related area. **Experience**: * Ideally, candidates will have some prior hedge fund documentation and client service exposure or have a paralegal or corporate administrator background. * Have a strong interest in the Financial Services Sector. * Outstanding time management, organizational skills, oral/written communication skills, critical thinking and attention to detail is required. * Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment including reporting and providing support to various members of the team. * Strong MS Office and Analytic Skills; mid\-level information management software experience required.

title: Junior Civil Engineer - Rail Systems
description: How about developing your professional career in a company where you can one day become a partner! Our fundamental values are customer satisfaction, our own people, and their professional development. If you think this is something that interests you, read on! **ABOUT THE JOB** We are looking for a Junior Civil Engineer for our Rail Systems Area for **participating in the biggest and most important railway project in Ireland** . There will be an initial period of 6 months of learning \& trainning in Spain (Bilbao or Madrid) being able to meet part of the team, and after that the location would be Dublin. At IDOM, we can offer you the opportunity to outdo yourself every day, developing your professional career while working on national and international projects, in an environment of collaboration and teamwork. You will be part of a multidisciplinary group where you will be a key player. You will get to experience different businesses and technologies in a flexible and supportive work setting. **If you join IDOM, you will find** * *A flexible timetable* * *Work\-life balance* * *Long\-term career path* * *Everyone has the possibility of becoming a partner* * *Multicultural and multidisciplinary environment* * *Possibility of taking on responsibility* * *Mutual commitment* * *Unifying Interpersonal relationships* **ABOUT YOU** **Apply if you...** * Have a degree in Civil or Industrial Engineering and interest in Railway projects. * Have the ability to work in a team and technical rigour. * Work autonomously and with an innovative mindset. * Are willing to travel and travel to other countries. * Knowledge of other languages apart from English is an asset (Spanish, French, German). * Above all... you are eager to learn in an international company like IDOM. **ABOUT US** IDOM describes itself as an Association of Professionals working in the areas of Engineering, Architecture and Consulting on projects that contribute to a more sustainable world. The ownership structure of IDOM creates a culture of engagement, to offer the best service for the client, while its people develop on both a professional and personal level. Therefore, IDOM's most important assets are the expertise and experience of its people. Highly qualified and skilled professionals, deeply united, working within a framework of professional liberty and integrity, with a strong commitment to progress, development, quality service and innovation. Since its foundation in 1957, the company has grown steadily and is now a multidisciplinary and integrated multinational organization, with more than 5,000 people working in over 130 countries. **Are you ready to take the next step?** If you join our TEAM, you will have the opportunity to develop your career to the highest level, in an environment of respect and professional integrity. If you share our philosophy and want to be part of our company, send us your CV. We are looking forward to meeting you!

title: Junior Consultant - Training Provided
description: Are you an ambitious professional looking to make a significant impact in a new and dynamic field? If you have a passion for people and thrive in fast\-paced environments, we have an exciting opportunity for you! * **Unlimited Earning Potential: Fast\-Paced \& Rewarding Environment** * **Clear and Transparent Career Development – Progress to Leadership** * **Personalised Training – Use your skills in a new, exciting way** **What can you expect day to day?** As an Associate Recruitment Consultant, you'll have the freedom to do things differently, supported by colleagues with a wealth of experience, empowering you to learn and grow. No day is the same here at Access, and you will gain experience across a varied range of skills including: * Building relationships with clients and candidates * Managing vacancies from start to finish * Screening, interviewing and shortlisting candidates in the healthcare industry Here at Access, you really can influence your own earning potential, by getting out what you put in. Your energy and dedication will be rewarded in a culture built on celebrating success. **What we are looking for** : * 1 – 2 years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) – No previous recruitment experience is needed. * Passion to succeed and be part of a fast\-paced inclusive environment * Relationship\-focused with the confidence to build strong networks * Collaborative in spirit with a growth mindset to develop a long\-lasting career **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission (Average OTE: Year 1 €45k, Year 2 €75k) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: until 12 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making (all leaders grow inside) * Paid sabbatical * Wellness programme (e.g. time management, skills development, health talks, advise and activities, stress management) * Industry\-leading training programme with 200\+ training modules – Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and team socials If you are curious enough to know more, all you need to do is send us a current CV! francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie ***Please note that only EU Citizens \& Stamp 4 applications will be considered for this role.***

title: Junior IT Operations Engineer
description: **What’s the Opportunity** At Distilled, we're looking for a **Junior IT Operations / L1 Helpdesk Engineer** to join our team and help deliver a fantastic technology experience for our employees. This is a hands\-on role focused on supporting our people, maintaining our office technology, and ensuring employees have the tools and support they need to do their best work. You'll work closely with the IT Operations team, providing day\-to\-day support across hardware, software, meeting rooms, onboarding, and office technology while developing your skills across modern cloud\-based platforms such as Google Workspace, Okta, Jamf, Microsoft 365 and Freshservice. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving problems, helping people, learning new technologies and taking ownership of their work. **Please note:** This role requires **4 days per week onsite** at our centrally located Dublin office, with flexibility to work remotely on the remaining day. **What will I be doing** As a Junior IT Operations Engineer, you'll play a key role in supporting employees and maintaining the smooth operation of our technology environment. You'll work across a wide variety of technologies while building your skills and experience in a supportive team environment. * **Employee Support \& Service Delivery:** Providing remote and onsite IT support for employees across the business, troubleshooting hardware, software, application and device issues and responding to both walk\-up requests and helpdesk tickets. Assisting employees with Google Workspace, Okta, Microsoft 365 and other business applications, maintaining high levels of customer service and communication throughout, and escalating issues appropriately when required. * **Onboarding, Offboarding \& User Lifecycle:** Preparing laptops and equipment for new starters, including purchasing and shipping as needed. Assisting with employee onboarding and offboarding activities, including creating and updating user accounts, groups and permissions. Ensuring devices, applications and access are ready for Day 1, while maintaining accurate records of employee equipment and access. * **Office Technology \& Meeting Rooms:** Maintaining meeting room technology, AV equipment and shared workspaces, supporting company meetings, presentations and events. Troubleshooting issues with displays, cameras, microphones and video conferencing systems, ensure hotdesks and shared technology areas remain operational and well maintained, and be a visible and approachable IT presence in the office. * **Asset Management \& Inventory:** Maintaining accurate IT asset inventory records and assisting with equipment ordering and stock management, tracking hardware throughout its lifecycle. Organise and maintain the IT stock room, ensuring spare equipment and accessories are available when needed. * **Continuous Learning \& Improvement:** Continuously developing skills across the technology stack and contributing to documentation and knowledge base articles. Identify recurring issues and suggest improvements, and support automation and process improvement initiatives where appropriate. **What skills and experience do I need** * Minimum 1 years’ experience in an IT Support, Service Desk, Desktop Support or IT Operations role * Strong troubleshooting skills across Windows, MacOS or Mobile devices * Experience supporting Microsoft 365, Google Workspace or similar collaboration platforms * Experience with user account management and onboarding/offboarding activities * Experience with supporting video conference room setup * Strong communication and customer service skills * Ability to prioritise workload and manage multiple tasks * Positive attitude and willingness to learn * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail * Comfortable working onsite and interacting directly with employees **Advantage if you have** * Exposure to Intune, Jamf, Okta, Entra ID or other identity/device management platforms * Experience with Google Meet or Zoom Rooms * Experience maintaining asset inventory or equipment lifecycle processes * Basic networking knowledge (Wi\-Fi, VPNs, DNS, DHCP) * Experience with Freshservice, or similar ticketing systems * ITIL Foundation, Jamf 100, Google Workspace, Microsoft or CompTIA certifications **What’s it like to work here** You will have the opportunity to work with one of Ireland's fastest growing Internet companies. You will get the chance to create a significant contribution to our strategic direction. Projects are exciting and employees are given wide scope for creativity and innovation **Benefits \- what you can expect** * Our goal is to maintain a culture where people look forward to work – whether in our centrally located office in Dublin or working from home. * We do that by making Distilled a place you'll be listened to and trusted to do your job. Where you can have fun, learn and feel appreciated. And where you get focused career development, growth and progression opportunities. * We want you to feel confident that this is the right place for you, your career, your wellbeing and your future. * Above all, your health and wellbeing are at the forefront of how we build the business. That means looking after you while you're here and later in life. So you'll receive life insurance, fully\-paid healthcare and a 5% contribution towards your pension. **More about Distilled** We exist to make buying and selling in Ireland simple. We put our people first, creating an inclusive environment where we can develop products that put our users first. We value people’s work and time, creating a community where they have an opportunity to make an impact and grow. Our challenge is to enhance our market\-leading products using insights and empathy to present our users with the perfect match. What we build is technology, but what we do is create connections for life’s most meaningful moments. We are Distilled and we **bring people together** . **Our Values** We have five core values: We have a **growth mindset.** We like to **simplify things** and **make things happen** . We **collaborate** across teams, business units and brands. We're **passionate** about what we do. These values give us a framework for leadership and daily decision making. They embody how we behave and present ourselves — as individuals and as a business — both internally and externally for our colleagues, our users and our customers. *We are a diverse bunch of people here in Distilled, and we want to continue to attract and retain a diverse range of people into our Company. We do not discriminate based on gender, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race and membership of the Traveller community.*

title: Junior Project Management Coordinator
description: We are adding a Junior Project Management Coordinator to our team to help us manage a series of technical projects for a partner in the international financial services sector. This role is about the practical side of delivery: making sure requirements are clear, keeping project tickets updated, and ensuring that our developers have exactly what they need to get their work done. This is a fully remote position, meaning you will work from your own space and coordinate with us through digital tools. **About Pixel Systems** Pixel Systems is a small software agency that builds the tools teams actually use. We do not build flashy social media apps or marketing landing pages. We focus on the practical stuff like admin panels, CRM integrations, and the database migrations that most teams try to avoid. Our clients come to us when they need to replace a sprawling spreadsheet with a real database or get different software tools to communicate with each other. We believe that working code is better than a perfect plan that never ships. Our approach is straightforward: we provide fixed quotes before we start, we do weekly demos, and we hand over everything when we are finished. This includes the code, the documentation, and even walkthrough videos for the client. We use stable, well\-tested technologies like Node, React, and Postgres because we want the software we build to be reliable. We are a small, distributed team that prefers solving real operational headaches over chasing the latest industry trends. **The Role** As a Junior Project Management Coordinator, your focus will be on the daily flow of information. You will be the person who translates client requests into clear tasks for our development team. Because our partner in the international financial services sector has precise data requirements, you will spend a lot of time organizing specifications and checking that nothing has been missed. Your work keeps our projects from getting bogged down in confusion or technical debt. On a typical day, you might be cleaning up a project board, assisting a lead developer with documentation, or helping to organize a weekly demo for a client. You will not be expected to write code, but you will be expected to understand how our systems work so you can talk about them accurately. This is a permanent, full\-time role where you will be an essential link between the client requirements and the final product. **What you bring** * A high level of organization and an obsession with lists and clear notes. * The ability to write instructions that are easy for others to read and follow. * A patient approach to repetitive tasks, such as updating ticket statuses or checking data consistency. * Comfort working in a fully remote environment using chat and video calls as your primary base for communication. * An interest in how software is built, even if you do not have a technical degree. * The ability to stay calm and helpful when a project timeline needs to be adjusted. * A direct and honest communication style: we prefer hearing about a problem early rather than late. * The capacity to manage your own time without someone looking over your shoulder. **Helpful background** * Experience using basic project tools like Trello, Jira, or similar task tracking software. * A basic understanding of spreadsheets and how to organize data within them. * Exposure to technical environments, perhaps through a bootcamp or personal project. * Knowledge of what an API is or how a database works at a conceptual level. * Experience in a customer service or administrative role where detail was important. * Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar document collaboration tools. **Working at Pixel Systems** Working here is different from working at a large tech company. We have no complex management layers and no office politics to navigate. We focus entirely on shipping work that helps real people, like operations teams and financial analysts. You will be working directly with senior developers and designers who value your input on how to make our internal processes smoother. Since we are 100 percent remote, we value clear, written communication above all else. We do not have meetings just for the sake of having them. Instead, we use our time to solve problems and get the work done. The position is paid, and we provide a level of compensation that is very competitive for entry\-level roles in the current market. You will have the opportunity to learn how a lean software agency operates from the inside out. We are a small team that values a quiet, focused work environment. We do not chase the latest tech fads or try to reinvent the wheel. We simply build stable systems that solve problems for our clients. If you enjoy bring order to chaos and helping a technical team stay on track, you will fit in well here.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: **Junior Quantity Surveyor – Civil Engineering Projects** A leading civil engineering contractor is seeking a Graduate Quantity Surveyor to join their growing commercial team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or an individual with up to one year's experience to develop their career within a well\-established and highly regarded contractor delivering major infrastructure projects across Dublin and surrounding areas. The successful candidate will work closely with senior commercial staff, gaining exposure to all aspects of quantity surveying on civil engineering and infrastructure projects. The role offers a combination of office and site\-based experience, supporting the delivery of projects across Dublin, Meath, and Kildare, with a flagship project based at Dublin Airport. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the preparation of project cost reports, valuations, and commercial documentation * Support the management and administration of subcontractor accounts * Assist in the preparation and assessment of subcontractor payment applications * Monitor project costs and help identify commercial risks and opportunities * Assist with the preparation and agreement of variations and final accounts * Maintain accurate commercial records and project documentation * Liaise with site management teams, subcontractors, suppliers, and clients * Support monthly cost reporting and forecasting processes * Assist with procurement activities and subcontractor packages * Ensure commercial procedures and contractual requirements are followed throughout the project lifecycle **Requirements** * Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related construction discipline * Ideally 1 year's experience in a Quantity Surveying role, although candidates with relevant work placement experience gained during college will also be considered * Previous experience within civil engineering projects is desirable * Candidates with a general construction background will also be considered * Strong numerical, analytical, and commercial awareness skills * Excellent communication and organisational abilities * Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team * Full driving licence required **What's on Offer** * Competitive salary (€40,000 – €45,000 DOE) * Company vehicle provided * Opportunity to work with a leading civil engineering contractor * Exposure to large\-scale and varied infrastructure projects, including Dublin Airport **To Apply** If you are a Graduate Quantity Surveyor or recent graduate seeking an opportunity within civil engineering and infrastructure projects, please contact Amy Maguire for a confidential discussion or send your CV. Email: a.maguire@gpcireland.com GPC will never send your CV to clients without your permission. GPC is a specialist construction and engineering recruitment consultancy based in Dublin with over 27 years’ experience placing professionals across Ireland and internationally.

title: Junior Sales Assistant
description: **Junior Sales Assistant** **Location:** Naas, Co. Kildare **Job Type:** Full\-Time, Permanent **Salary:** Competitive (Based on Experience) **About Us** At N7 Commercial Centre, we are a growing and dynamic business specialising in commercial vehicles, horseboxes, fleet solutions, and workshop services. Due to continued expansion, we are seeking a motivated and enthusiastic **Junior Sales Assistant** to join our team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to start or develop a career in sales within a fast\-paced and supportive environment. **The Role** As a Junior Sales Assistant, you will support the sales team in delivering exceptional customer service while assisting with vehicle sales enquiries, lead generation, and administrative tasks. **Key Responsibilities** * Assisting customers with sales enquiries in person, over the phone, and via email. * Supporting the sales team with quotations and customer follow\-ups. * Maintaining customer records and updating CRM systems. * Assisting with social media and online vehicle advertisements. * Preparing vehicle documentation and sales packs. * Helping to maintain showroom and forecourt presentation. * Building positive relationships with new and existing customers. * Supporting marketing and promotional activities as required. **Requirements** **Essential** * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. * Good computer literacy (Microsoft Office). * Positive attitude and willingness to learn. * Full clean driving licence. * Ability to work as part of a team. **Desirable** * Previous customer service or sales experience. * Interest in commercial vehicles, automotive, agricultural, or equestrian industries. * Experience using CRM systems. **What We Offer** Full training provided Supportive team environment On\-site parking Opportunity to grow within a successful and expanding company **How to Apply** Please submit your CV along with a short cover letter outlining why you would be a great fit for the role. We look forward to hearing from you. Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence (in our brand new office!) and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

title: Junior Technical Analyst - Aviation Advisory
description: **Job Description** The Aviation Advisory team founded in 2016 has since established itself as best in class across its service offerings, growing to a team of 60\+ staff in just 8 years. Current client offerings include; aircraft lease management, data migration management, aircraft maintenance reserve reviews, technical aircraft expertise, deal appraisals for investment decision making, and a broad range of accounting and finance offerings to its clients. The team is currently undertaking lease management for a fleet size of approximately 1,400 leases and provides professional services to 13 out of the Top 15 Aircraft Lessors. **The Opportunity** We have an opportunity for an entry level technical analyst, who is interested in working within the GT Aviation Lease Services team. The successful candidate will be joining a team that has gone through significant growth in recent years with ambitious plans to grow the Aviation Lease Services offering. This is an opportunity for someone with the ability to understand problems quickly to work with the number one provider of Aviation Lease Services to the aircraft leasing industry in Dublin – you will be immersed in the industry with high calibre colleagues. **Roles \& Responsibilities** * Fleet technical records data management, documentation updating and control * Assist with asset review system control and assessments * Manage and administer the technical specification database and reporting * Input monthly utilization and review component status changes * Maintain up to date technical knowledge of each aircraft by type * Input maintain and review fleet’s aircraft specifications, aircraft technical information, equipment data and specifications * Analyzing and updating shop visit events * Assist with MR Claims process **Job Requirements** * Aeronautical Engineering, Aviation Technology Degree or Equivalent * Detail\-Orientated and organized * Meet deadlines and be able to prioritize * Must be diligent, thorough, numerate with a keen attention to detail * Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word, Outlook * Previous experience of working in aviation is a distinct advantage **About Us** **We are Grant Thornton** Grant Thornton Ireland is rapidly approaching 3,000 people, in 9 offices across Ireland, Isle of Man, Gibraltar and Bermuda. With a presence in over 149 countries around the world and a global network of 73,000 people, we bring our clients the local knowledge, national expertise and global presence to help them succeed – wherever they’re located. At GT, we work as ***trusted advisors*** , bringing local knowledge and national expertise, with a global presence, to help businesses succeed – wherever they are located. We make business more personal by investing in building relationships and empowering our clients to make the right decisions for their organisation now and for the future. Whether that is working with the public sector to build thriving communities, with regulators and financial institutions to build trust, or with a diverse range of businesses to help them achieve their goals, Grant Thornton Ireland work hard to support clients to act on the issues that matter. **At GT Ireland we don’t just predict your future, we build it** . **A Career at GT** Looking for a more fulfilling role in professional services? One where fresh thinking, collaboration and diversity are valued? At Grant Thornton we do things differently. **What does this mean for you?** A career in a more inclusive working environment, a more collaborative work culture, a more supported, flexible working role, more possibilities to grow and more opportunities to help shape the future for your clients. We respect and value your experience. And we want you to bring your authentic self to work and be at your best. It is how it should be. **Grow with us** At Grant Thornton, we care about our people and work hard to make you feel valued. If you are looking to deepen and develop your skills, knowledge, and experience throughout your career, then that is what you will get, and more. **Our Benefits** Please follow this link for information on our generous benefits package. **About The Team** **Equity, diversity and inclusion** At Grant Thornton, we provide equitable opportunities for all our colleagues. We are a responsible, sustainable business where equity, diversity and inclusion (ED\&I) is at the forefront of our workplace culture agenda, and today, we continue to build and develop on our existing ED\&I structure and strategy to meet our workplace culture needs. People are at the heart of our business and teams built with varied individuals present diverse viewpoints, which need to be heard and valued. We are all at our best when we are able to be ourselves and we view integrity and authenticity as integral values to bring to our day\-to\-day work\-life at the firm. We are excited to see the personality and perspectives you will bring to our team because we know we will all benefit from them. Diversity of thought, background and experience enables better decision\-making, improves the quality of our delivery, and helps us to meet the needs of our clients. Our firm is built on people and their ideas, so we want to hear all the new perspectives and fresh thinking you have to offer. You form the bedrock of our firm’s best\-practice principles and we will champion you as leaders from day one. **Reward And Benefits** Our reward and benefits are designed to create an environment where our people can flourish. We are committed to building a culture where our people have access to the necessary benefits to help promote a healthy lifestyle and thrive. **Recognition** We want to create a culture of recognition and celebrating success, by saying thank you to people who surpass our expectations and recognising the right values and behaviours. Our Shout Out recognition scheme is our way of highlighting and promoting achievements. Whether you simply want to say thank you, celebrate a special occasion or give an award for doing something exceptional, you can do all of this and more through the scheme.

This is an opportunity for someone with the ability to understand problems quickly to work with the number one provider of Aviation Lease Services to the aircraft leasing industry in Dublin – you will be immersed in the industry with high calibre colleagues. **Roles \& Responsibilities** * Fleet technical records data management, documentation updating and control * Assist with asset review system control and assessments * Manage and administer the technical specification database and reporting * Input monthly utilization and review component status changes * Maintain up to date technical knowledge of each aircraft by type * Input maintain and review fleet’s aircraft specifications, aircraft technical information, equipment data and specifications * Analyzing and updating shop visit events * Assist with MR Claims process **Job Requirements** * Aeronautical Engineering, Aviation Technology Degree or Equivalent * Detail\-Orientated and organized * Meet deadlines and be able to prioritize * Must be diligent, thorough, numerate with a keen attention to detail * Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word, Outlook * Previous experience of working in aviation is a distinct advantage

title: KCAT Administrator

Management * Supporting with reporting (weekly/monthly and ad hoc where required) * Attending team meetings – managing meeting minutes and actions required * Ensure that all procedures and processes are adhered to * Undertake any reasonable role related duties which may be required from time to time. **Knowledge, Skills \& Experience** * Must have at least 1 years’ experience in an administrative business * High attention to detail and a focus on accuracy * Comfortable using Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint and Outlook) * Enjoys working as part of a team and contributing to a pressure * Experience in the motor or fleet industry is beneficial, but not essential **Why Ayvens?** With over 3\.4 million vehicles managed across more than 42 countries, we provide full\-service
